Identifier of the billing cycle during which the remote service order was executed, used for meter-to-cash reconciliation and revenue assurance.

Foreign key linking to renewable.der_registry. Business justification: Remote disconnect/reconnect commands for DER sites during emergency curtailment events, interconnection violations, or VPP dispatch. Emergency curtailment dispatch process requires remote service orde

Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Remote commands to DER meters (export curtailment, disconnect, firmware update) must reference the DER system to enforce interconnection agreement export limits and protection settings. Grid operators
Identifier of the demand response event that triggered the remote service order, if the order is part of a load management or curtailment program.

Foreign key linking to billing.dunning_notice. Business justification: Disconnect remote service orders are directly triggered by dunning notices at a specific dunning level. This FK links the physical AMI disconnect command to the dunning notice that initiated it, requi

Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Before executing remote disconnect/reconnect or load-limiting orders on large C&I customers, grid operators assess EMS node loading impact. NERC reliability standards require load impact assessment be
Identifier of the service point associated with the meter receiving the remote service action.

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Remote disconnect/reconnect orders must comply with regulatory obligations — consumer protection rules, disconnection moratoriums, advance notice requirements, and medical baseline protections. remote
Identifier of the smart meter on which the remote service action was executed.

Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: Remote disconnect/reconnect commands coordinate with SCADA-controlled distribution switching for load management, demand response, and safety interlocks. Distribution automation integration requiremen
FK to metering.meter.meter_id — Remote service orders (connect/disconnect/on-demand read) must reference the target meter device. Core operational link for AMI remote operations.

Foreign key linking to renewable.vpp_configuration. Business justification: VPP dispatch operations issue remote service orders to DER meters for load control and generation dispatch. Linking remote_service_order to vpp_configuration enables VPP dispatch audit trails and perf
Identifier of the associated field work order if the remote service order was initiated as part of a broader field service activity (e.g., meter installation, maintenance).
Date and time when the remote service order was actually executed on the meter, recorded by the AMI head-end system upon completion.
Unique transaction identifier assigned by the AMI head-end system for tracking the command execution at the network layer.
Technical payload or command string sent to the meter by the AMI head-end system, stored for audit and troubleshooting purposes.
Communication protocol used by the AMI network to transmit the remote service command to the meter (e.g., ZigBee, cellular, RF mesh, power line carrier, Wi-Fi).
Date and time when the remote service order reached a terminal state (success, failed, cancelled, or timeout) and was closed in the system.
Date and time when the remote service order record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.
Indicates whether a customer notification (email, SMS, IVR call) was sent prior to or after the remote service order execution, as required by regulatory or business policy.
Date and time when the customer notification was sent regarding the remote service order, used for regulatory compliance documentation.
Business reason for a remote disconnect order, supporting meter-to-cash workflows and regulatory compliance reporting (e.g., non-payment, customer request, safety hazard).
Standardized code returned by the AMI head-end system indicating the outcome of the remote service order execution (e.g., SUCCESS, METER_OFFLINE, COMMAND_REJECTED, TIMEOUT).
Detailed code explaining why the remote service order failed, populated only when order_status is failed; used for root cause analysis and retry logic.
Human-readable description of the failure reason, providing additional context for troubleshooting and customer communication.
Target meter firmware version to be installed by the over-the-air (OTA) update, verified upon successful completion.
Meter firmware version installed before the over-the-air (OTA) update, used for version control and rollback tracking.
Date and time when the remote service order record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.
Power threshold in kilowatts (kW) set for load limiting activation orders, restricting customer consumption to prevent overload or support demand response programs.
Maximum number of retry attempts allowed for this remote service order before it is marked as permanently failed.
Date and time when the meter reading was captured at the meter during an on-demand read remote service order.
Unit of measure for the meter reading value captured during on-demand read (e.g., kWh for energy, kW for demand, kVAh for apparent energy).
Meter register reading value captured during an on-demand read remote service order, used for billing validation or dispute resolution.
Free-text notes or comments entered by users or systems regarding the remote service order, capturing additional context, special instructions, or resolution details.
Externally visible unique business identifier for the remote service order, used for tracking and customer communication.

Current lifecycle status of the remote service order: pending (queued), in progress (executing), success (completed successfully), failed (execution error), cancelled (user/system cancelled), or timeout (no response from meter).
Type of remote service action executed via AMI: remote connect, remote disconnect, on-demand meter read, load limiting activation/deactivation, or firmware over-the-air (OTA) update.

Priority level assigned to the remote service order, determining execution sequence in the AMI command queue (critical for safety/emergency, high for revenue protection, normal for routine, low for bulk operations).
Business reason for a remote reconnect order, typically following payment receipt or resolution of the disconnect condition.
Indicates whether the remote service order execution complies with applicable Public Utility Commission (PUC) regulations for disconnect/reconnect procedures and customer notification requirements.
Date and time when the remote service order was requested by the system or user, representing the business event initiation time.
Name or identifier of the source system that initiated the remote service order (e.g., CC&B, OMS, MDM, manual CSR entry).
Technical response payload returned by the meter to the AMI head-end system, stored for audit and troubleshooting purposes.
Number of times the remote service order has been retried after initial failure, used to track execution persistence and prevent infinite retry loops.
Date and time when the remote service order is scheduled to be executed on the meter, may differ from requested timestamp for planned actions.
Signal strength in decibels-milliwatts (dBm) measured at the time of remote service order execution, used to diagnose communication failures.
Unique identifier for the TOU or CPP rate program. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to distribution.load_zone. Business justification: TOU rate programs are designed around load zone peak demand characteristics for grid load shaping. Utility rate design and demand response teams use this link to associate TOU programs with target loa

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: TOU rate programs are established under specific regulatory obligations (state RPS mandates, demand response program rules, commission-ordered rate design). Linking tou_rate_program to its governing o

Foreign key linking to billing.rate_schedule. Business justification: TOU rate programs implement specific rate schedule TOU components (peak/off-peak periods, CPP triggers). This FK links the program definition to the governing rate schedule, required for billing valid

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: TOU programs require commission approval via tariff filing or rate case. tou_rate_program has denormalized regulatory_approval_reference and tariff_sheet_reference text fields. Replacing these with a

FK to metering.meter_point.meter_point_id — After merging tou_enrollment into tou_rate_program, the enrollment association linking programs to meter points must be maintained. Essential for billing domain to apply correct TOU periods.

business_glossary_term: Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) Meter Required Flag
description: Indicates whether an AMI smart meter with interval data capability is required for enrollment in this program. True if AMI is mandatory; False if standard meters are acceptable.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Meter Configuration Requirements

description: Indicates whether enrollment effective dates must align with billing cycle boundaries. True if enrollment can only start at the beginning of a billing cycle.
reference: Oracle Utilities CC&B Billing Cycle Configuration

description: Email address for inquiries about the rate program. Used by customer service representatives and external stakeholders.
Phone number for inquiries about the rate program. Used by customer service representatives and external stakeholders.

business_glossary_term: Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) Event Trigger Criteria
description: Business rules or thresholds that trigger a CPP event (e.g., 'Forecasted system load >95% capacity', 'Day-ahead LMP >$150/MWh', 'Heat index >105F'). Nullable for non-CPP programs.
Maximum duration in hours for a single CPP event. Nullable for non-CPP programs.
Maximum number of CPP events that can be called in a calendar year, as defined in the tariff. Nullable for non-CPP programs.
Minimum advance notice in hours that must be provided to customers before a CPP event begins. Nullable for non-CPP programs.
Timestamp when this rate program record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.
Customer class or segment eligible for this rate program. Defines which customer types can enroll.
Date when the rate program expires or is superseded. Nullable for open-ended programs. After this date, no new enrollments are accepted.
reference: State PUC Rate Tariff Filing Requirements

description: Date when the rate program becomes effective and available for customer enrollment. Aligns with regulatory approval effective date.
reference: State PUC Rate Tariff Filing Requirements

description: Detailed criteria that customers must meet to be eligible for enrollment (e.g., 'Residential customers with annual usage >5000 kWh', 'Commercial customers with demand >50 kW').
reference: State PUC Rate Program Enrollment Rules

description: Defines how customers are enrolled in the program. OPT_IN: customer must actively enroll; OPT_OUT: customer is enrolled by default but can opt out; DEFAULT: automatic enrollment for new customers; MANDATORY: required for customer segment.
reference: State PUC Rate Program Enrollment Rules

description: Estimated average annual cost savings in US dollars for a typical customer enrolled in this program. Used for program marketing and benefit analysis.
Estimated average annual energy savings in kilowatt-hours (kWh) for a typical customer enrolled in this program. Used for program marketing and benefit analysis.
Code identifying the source system or integration point for this program record (e.g., 'MDM', 'CC&B', 'CIS'). Used for data lineage and system-of-record tracking.
Timestamp when this rate program record was last updated. Audit trail for record lifecycle and change tracking.
Reference code linking this rate program to marketing campaigns and customer acquisition initiatives. Nullable if no active marketing campaign.
Minimum number of months a customer must remain enrolled in the program before they can opt out or switch to another rate. Nullable if no minimum commitment.
reference: State PUC Rate Program Enrollment Rules

description: Minimum frequency in minutes at which interval data must be collected for this program (e.g., 15, 30, 60). Defines the granularity required for TOU period classification.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Interval Data Standards

description: User ID or system account that last modified this rate program record. Audit trail for accountability and compliance.
Textual or structured definition of off-peak period hours (e.g., 'All other hours', 'Weekdays 8pm-2pm, All day weekends'). Defines when off-peak TOU rates apply.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Rate Period Configuration

description: Indicates whether customers are allowed to opt out of the program after enrollment. False for mandatory programs; True for voluntary programs.
reference: State PUC Rate Program Enrollment Rules

description: Number of days advance notice required for a customer to opt out of the program. Nullable if opt-out is not allowed or has no notice requirement.
reference: State PUC Rate Program Enrollment Rules

description: Textual or structured definition of peak period hours (e.g., 'Weekdays 2pm-8pm', 'Mon-Fri 14:00-20:00'). Defines when peak TOU rates apply.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Rate Period Configuration

description: Business identifier for the rate program, used in billing systems and customer communications. Externally-known unique code for this TOU or CPP program.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Program Code Standard

description: Detailed business description of the rate program, including objectives, benefits, and customer value proposition. Used in customer communications and marketing materials.
reference: Oracle Utilities CC&B Program Documentation Standards

description: Human-readable name of the TOU or CPP rate program (e.g., 'Residential Time-of-Use Summer 2024', 'Commercial Critical Peak Pricing').
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Program Naming Convention

description: Business unit or department responsible for managing and administering this rate program (e.g., 'Metering Operations', 'Demand Response', 'Regulatory Affairs').

Current lifecycle status of the rate program. DRAFT: under development; PENDING_APPROVAL: submitted to PUC; ACTIVE: available for enrollment; SUSPENDED: temporarily inactive; RETIRED: no longer offered; CANCELLED: terminated before activation.
reference: State PUC Rate Program Lifecycle Standards

description: Classification of the rate program structure: TOU (Time-of-Use with fixed peak/off-peak periods), CPP (Critical Peak Pricing with event-driven pricing), TOU_CPP_HYBRID (combination), DYNAMIC_PRICING, or REAL_TIME_PRICING.
reference: NAESB Retail Electric Quadrant Business Practice Standards

description: Date when the regulatory body (PUC) approved this rate program. Critical for compliance and audit trails.
reference: State PUC Rate Tariff Filing Requirements

description: Defines whether the program applies year-round or only during specific seasons. SEASONAL_VARIABLE indicates different rate periods apply in different seasons.
Textual or structured definition of shoulder (mid-peak) period hours, if applicable. Nullable if program has only peak/off-peak structure.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Rate Period Configuration

description: Month and day when summer season ends (MM-DD format, e.g., '09-30' for September 30th). Applicable when season_type includes summer periods.
Month and day when summer season begins (MM-DD format, e.g., '06-01' for June 1st). Applicable when season_type includes summer periods.

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Rule Set
description: Reference to the VEE rule set applied to interval data for this program. Defines validation, estimation, and editing processes for data quality assurance.

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Exception Identifier
description: Unique identifier for the VEE exception record. Primary key for the VEE exception entity.
Reference to the customer account associated with the meter and service point for this VEE exception. Used for customer impact analysis and billing hold workflows.

Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: VEE exceptions identified as audit findings when indicating systematic data quality issues or control deficiencies. Supports audit evidence collection, corrective action tracking, regulatory reporting

Foreign key linking to renewable.curtailment_event. Business justification: VEE exceptions on DER interval data are frequently caused by curtailment events producing anomalous readings. Linking enables automated VEE resolution by identifying curtailment as root cause — a stan

Foreign key linking to demand.dr_event. Business justification: VEE exceptions during DR event windows require context to determine if anomalous readings are caused by legitimate load curtailment rather than data errors. Linking vee_exception to the active dr_even

Foreign key linking to outage.outage_event. Business justification: VEE analysts investigate whether missing/anomalous interval readings stem from power interruptions. Regulatory VEE reports require cross-referencing exceptions with outage events to justify estimation

Foreign key linking to trading.market_settlement. Business justification: VEE exceptions that correct interval data after initial settlement require settlement restatement. ISO tariffs mandate tracking which market_settlement records are affected by data quality corrections
Reference to the meter that generated the interval data which triggered this VEE exception.
Reference to the service point (delivery location) associated with the meter that generated the interval data for this exception. Used to link exceptions to customer accounts and premises.
Reference to the specific interval data record that failed VEE validation and raised this exception.

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Rule Identifier
description: Reference to the VEE rule that was triggered and caused this exception to be raised.

Reference to the rate schedule (tariff) applicable to the service point at the time of the exception. Used to determine billing impact and priority for resolution, especially for Time-of-Use (TOU) and Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) customers.

FK to metering.interval_reading.interval_reading_id — VEE exceptions must reference the interval reading(s) that failed validation. Without this link, operators cannot identify and resolve data quality issues.
FK to metering.vee_rule.vee_rule_id — Each VEE exception must reference the VEE rule that triggered it. This is essential for exception resolution workflows and rule tuning.

Boolean flag indicating whether the VEE engine attempted automatic resolution of this exception through estimation algorithms. True if auto-resolution was attempted; False if exception was immediately flagged for manual review.

Boolean flag indicating whether automatic resolution by the VEE engine was successful. True if the engine successfully estimated a replacement value that passed validation; False if auto-resolution failed and manual intervention is required.

Boolean flag indicating whether this VEE exception affects customer billing. True if the affected interval data is used in billing determinants and the exception must be resolved before bill calculation; False if the exception is for non-billing data (e.g., operational monitoring, grid analytics).

Final corrected interval data value (in kWh or other unit of measure) after manual review and editing. This is the value that will be used for billing if the exception is resolved. Null if not yet corrected.
Timestamp when this VEE exception record was first created in the MDM system. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.

Numeric data quality score (0.00 to 100.00) assigned to the interval data by the VEE engine, indicating confidence in the data accuracy. Lower scores trigger exceptions. Used for trending meter health and VEE rule tuning.
Estimated interval data value (in kWh or other unit of measure) calculated by the VEE engine to replace the invalid or missing original value. Null if estimation was not performed or failed.

Method used by the VEE engine or analyst to estimate the corrected interval value. Linear interpolation: average of adjacent intervals. Historical average: average of same interval from prior days. Similar day: value from comparable day (same day-of-week, season). Regression: statistical model. Manual entry: analyst-provided value. None: no estimation performed.

Final disposition category of the VEE exception indicating how it was ultimately handled. Resolved: data corrected and validated. Escalated: sent to engineering or management for further investigation. Waived: accepted without correction per approved business rule or regulatory guidance.

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Exception Number
description: Business-facing unique exception number assigned to this VEE exception for tracking and audit purposes. Format: VEE-xxxxxxxxxx.
Timestamp when the VEE exception was first raised by the MDM VEE engine during interval data validation processing. Represents the business event time of exception detection.

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Exception Severity
description: Severity level of the VEE exception indicating business impact and urgency for resolution. Critical exceptions block billing; high exceptions require immediate review; medium and low exceptions are tracked for quality metrics; informational exceptions are logged for audit only.

Source system that originated the interval data which triggered this VEE exception. Typically MDM (Meter Data Management) for AMI data, AMI head-end for direct meter reads, SCADA for grid operations data, manual entry for field reads, or billing system for estimated reads.

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Exception Status
description: Current lifecycle status of the VEE exception. Open: newly raised and awaiting review. In Review: assigned to analyst for investigation. Resolved: corrective action taken and data corrected. Escalated: requires supervisor or engineering review. Waived: exception accepted without correction per business rule. Closed: final disposition recorded and exception archived.

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Exception Type
description: Classification of the VEE exception indicating the nature of the data quality issue: validation failure (data failed validation rule), estimation failure (unable to estimate missing interval), editing failure (manual edit rejected), missing data (no interval data received), suspect data (data flagged as questionable), or out of range (value exceeds expected bounds).
Number of individual interval readings affected by this VEE exception. For 15-minute interval meters, a 1-hour exception would affect 4 intervals.
End timestamp of the interval data range affected by this VEE exception. Defines the end of the time window containing suspect or invalid data.
Start timestamp of the interval data range affected by this VEE exception. Defines the beginning of the time window containing suspect or invalid data.
Timestamp when this VEE exception record was last updated. Audit field for tracking changes to exception status, resolution actions, and data corrections.
Original raw interval data value (in kWh or other unit of measure) that triggered the VEE exception before any estimation or editing was applied.

Boolean flag indicating whether this VEE exception must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., state Public Utility Commission) as part of data quality and billing accuracy compliance reporting. True if reportable; False otherwise.

Action taken to resolve the VEE exception. Re-estimate: apply different estimation algorithm. Manual edit: analyst manually corrected the value. Accept original: original value accepted despite exception. Override: supervisor approved exception without correction. Escalate: sent to engineering for investigation. Waive: exception acknowledged but no correction required per business rule.
Free-text notes entered by the analyst or system documenting the resolution action, rationale for the decision, and any additional context for audit purposes.

Timestamp when the VEE exception was resolved (status changed to resolved, waived, or closed). Null if exception is still open or in review. Used to calculate exception aging and VEE team performance metrics.
Full name of the analyst or system user who reviewed and resolved this VEE exception. Captured for audit trail and operational reporting.

Numeric threshold value defined in the VEE rule that was exceeded or violated, triggering this exception. For example, maximum expected consumption in kWh, or maximum percent deviation from historical average.

Unit of measure for the interval data values associated with this exception. Typically kWh (kilowatt-hour) for electric energy consumption, kW for demand, or gas volume units. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kWh|MWh|kW|kVAR|kVA|CCF|MCF|therms — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Batch Identifier
description: Reference to the VEE processing batch run that raised this exception. Used to group exceptions by processing cycle for operational dashboards and batch-level quality metrics.

business_glossary_term: Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) Rule Identifier
description: Unique identifier for the VEE rule. Primary key for the VEE rule reference entity.

Foreign key linking to asset.class. Business justification: VEE validation rules apply to specific meter asset classes (e.g., different estimation methods for residential vs. industrial CT meters). asset.class is the authoritative meter classification taxonomy

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: VEE rules implement regulatory obligations for data quality, estimation limits, validation thresholds mandated by state PUC orders. Enables compliance mapping, regulatory justification for VEE algorit

Foreign key linking to billing.rate_schedule. Business justification: VEE rules are scoped to specific rate schedules (TOU validation rules apply only to TOU rate schedules). The plain applicable_rate_schedules column on vee_rule is a denormalization signal. This FK ena

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: VEE rules implementing FERC Order 676 or state PUC data quality standards are established through regulatory filings. vee_rule has regulatory_basis (plain text denormalization). Replacing with regulat

The computational algorithm or method used by the rule to validate, estimate, or edit meter data (e.g., threshold-based, statistical analysis, linear regression, interpolation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: threshold|statistical|regression|interpolation|substitution|comparison|pattern_matching — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Type of utility service to which this VEE rule applies (electric, gas, water, steam). Ensures rules are applied to appropriate commodity data streams.
Indicates whether changes to this VEE rule require formal approval before activation. True for rules impacting billing or regulatory compliance.
User identifier of the person who approved this VEE rule for production use. Applicable when approval_required_flag is true.
Timestamp when this VEE rule was formally approved for production use. Supports governance and compliance documentation.

business_glossary_term: VEE Rule Audit Trail Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether detailed audit logging is enabled for this rule, capturing every application of the rule and its outcomes for compliance and troubleshooting.
Indicates whether data passing this VEE rule is considered billing-quality and suitable for revenue calculation. True if the rule ensures billing-grade accuracy.
Minimum confidence level (as a percentage) required for estimated values to be accepted as billing-quality. Used in statistical estimation algorithms.
Timestamp when this VEE rule record was first created in the MDM system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
Date after which the VEE rule is no longer active. Nullable for open-ended rules. Supports rule lifecycle management and historical analysis.
Date from which the VEE rule becomes active and is applied to meter data. Supports temporal rule management and regulatory compliance.

Specific estimation technique used when the rule type is estimation. Defines how missing or invalid interval data is calculated (e.g., linear interpolation, historical average, same-day-last-week). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_interpolation|historical_average|same_day_last_week|same_day_last_year|regression|zero_fill|prior_value — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Action taken when the rule detects a data quality exception: flag for review, automatically estimate, reject the data, route to manual review, or substitute with a default value.
Frequency at which the VEE rule is executed against incoming or historical meter data (real-time, hourly, daily, weekly, on-demand).

business_glossary_term: VEE Rule Last Modified By User
description: User identifier of the person who last modified this VEE rule record. Supports change tracking and accountability.
Timestamp when this VEE rule record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change management.

business_glossary_term: VEE Rule Lookback Period in Days
description: Number of days of historical data the rule examines for validation or estimation purposes. Used by statistical and pattern-matching algorithms.
Minimum acceptable value for validation rules. Interval data below this limit triggers a validation exception or editing action.
Free-text field for additional notes, implementation guidance, or special considerations related to this VEE rule. Supports knowledge transfer and operational documentation.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Business Practice Standards

description: Business unit or team responsible for maintaining and governing this VEE rule (e.g., Metering Operations, Revenue Assurance, Data Quality Team).
Execution sequence priority for the rule within the VEE processing engine. Lower numbers execute first. Determines the order in which multiple rules are applied to the same interval data.
Functional category of the rule indicating the specific data quality issue it addresses (e.g., spike detection, zero consumption validation, missing data estimation).
Business identifier code for the VEE rule, used for external reference and system integration. Typically a short alphanumeric code following utility naming conventions.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Business Practice Standards

description: Detailed description of the VEE rule including its business logic, application context, and expected outcomes when applied to interval meter data.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Business Practice Standards

description: Human-readable name of the VEE rule describing its purpose and function within the meter data management process.
Current lifecycle status of the VEE rule indicating whether it is actively applied to meter data processing or in a non-operational state.
Classification of the rule within the VEE process: validation (checks data quality), estimation (fills missing data), or editing (corrects erroneous data).

business_glossary_term: VEE Rule Threshold Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measure for the threshold value (e.g., kWh, MWh, percentage, count, standard deviations) providing context for threshold interpretation.
Numeric threshold parameter used by the rule algorithm to determine data quality exceptions. For example, spike tolerance percentage, maximum consumption limit, or minimum expected value.
Maximum acceptable value for validation rules. Interval data above this limit triggers a validation exception or editing action.
Version number of the VEE rule configuration. Incremented with each modification to support change tracking and rollback capabilities.
User identifier of the person who created this VEE rule record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.
Unique surrogate identifier for the affected customer association record linking a customer service point to an outage or planned maintenance event.

Foreign key linking to interconnection.interconnection_agreement. Business justification: Customer interruption records for DER customers must reference interconnection agreements for regulatory reporting of DER availability, NEM credit calculations during outages, and compliance with PUC

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_service_agreement. Business justification: Affected customer records need service agreement link to determine applicable credit/adjustment rules, verify eligibility for outage compensation programs, and ensure credits are posted to correct bil
Reference to the crew dispatch or work order assigned to restore power at this customer service point. Links to the field crew assignment in the OMS or Work Management System.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Ventyx Asset Suite Work Management

description: Reference to the customer account record in the Customer Information System (CIS). Sourced from SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B customer account master.

Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Tracks DER-owning customers experiencing outages for NEM credit adjustments (energy not exported during outage), performance guarantee calculations, and regulatory reporting (CPUC Rule 21 requires tra
Identifier of the substation serving this customer service point at the time of the outage. Used for substation-level reliability performance reporting and asset management correlation.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; GE PowerOn ADMS; Ventyx Asset Suite

description: Reference to the parent outage or planned maintenance event that caused this customer interruption. Links to the outage event record in the Outage Management System (OMS).
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; NERC TADS

description: Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit serving this customer service point at the time of the outage. Used for feeder-level reliability analysis and SAIDI/SAIFI segmentation.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; GE PowerOn ADMS; IEEE 1366

description: Foreign key linking to outage.interruption. Business justification: The affected_customer table and interruption table both track customer-level outage impacts but are not directly linked. The interruption table captures granular premise-level interruption records (du
Reference to the meter installed at the affected service point. Used to correlate outage impact with Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) last-gasp signals and meter data.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM; SAP IS-U Device Management

description: Reference to the switching order or planned maintenance work order that caused this customer interruption. Populated for planned outages; null for unplanned events.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Ventyx Asset Suite Work Management

description: Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: The affected_customer table links customers to outage events but has no direct FK to planned_outage_window. For planned outages, customers are notified in advance and their interruption records are tr

Foreign key linking to customer.premise. Business justification: Affected customer records must be tied to specific service premises for premise-level SAIDI/SAIFI calculations, life support flag verification, and premise-based restoration prioritization. affected_c
Reference to the specific service point (premise delivery point) associated with this customer interruption. Corresponds to the service point or premise identifier in the CIS and GIS.
reference: SAP IS-U Device Management; Esri ArcGIS Utility Network

description: Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: The affected_customer table has storm_event_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the customer was affected during a storm, but no FK to the storm_event master record. Direct linkage to storm_event enables storm-

Reference identifier for the customer trouble call or service request that reported this outage, as recorded in the Customer Information System (CIS) or CRM. Populated when customer_reported_flag is True.
reference: Oracle CC&B; SAP IS-U CIS; GE PowerOn ADMS OMS

description: The externally-known customer account number as assigned by the Customer Information System (CIS). Used for customer-facing communications and regulatory impact reporting.
reference: SAP IS-U CIS; Oracle CC&B; PUC Reporting Requirements

description: Number of hours of advance notice provided to this customer prior to a planned interruption. Used to verify compliance with PUC minimum advance notification requirements for planned outages (typically 48–72 hours).
reference: PUC Planned Outage Notification Rules; State PUC Regulations

description: Standardized cause code classifying the root cause of the outage affecting this customer (e.g., equipment failure, tree contact, animal contact, weather, vehicle accident). Sourced from OMS cause code taxonomy aligned with EEI/NERC reporting categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|tree_contact|animal_contact|weather|vehicle_accident|scheduled_maintenance|unknown|other — promote to reference product]
reference: NERC TADS Cause Codes; EEI Reliability Reporting; GE PowerOn ADMS OMS

description: The date and time when this affected customer association record was first created in the system, typically when the outage event was declared and customer impact was identified by the OMS.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Internal Data Governance Standards

description: Indicates whether the customer at this service point is registered as a critical care or life support customer (e.g., medical baseline, home oxygen, dialysis). Critical care customers receive priority restoration and mandatory advance notification per PUC regulations.
reference: PUC Medical Baseline Rules; CPUC Rule 14; State PUC Regulations

description: Specific type of critical care or life support designation for the affected customer. Populated only when critical_care_flag is True. Classified as restricted health information per HIPAA and state PUC medical baseline program rules.
reference: HIPAA; PUC Medical Baseline Program; State PUC Regulations

description: Regulatory and rate classification of the customer affected by the outage. Used for segmented reliability reporting and prioritization of restoration efforts per PUC requirements.
reference: PUC Rate Classification; SAP IS-U CIS; Oracle CC&B

description: Indicates whether this outage was identified through a customer trouble call or self-reported via web/app, as opposed to being detected automatically via AMI last-gasp, SCADA, or OMS prediction. Used for outage detection source analysis.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Oracle CC&B CRM

description: Identifies the operational source system from which this affected customer record was ingested into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit purposes.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Indicates whether the affected customer has a registered Distributed Energy Resource (DER) such as rooftop solar, battery storage, or a Net Energy Metering (NEM) arrangement. Relevant for assessing behind-the-meter (BTM) resilience and grid impact during outages.
reference: FERC Order 2222; DERMS Integration; NEM Program Rules

description: The estimated date and time of power restoration communicated to this customer. Corresponds to the Estimated Restoration Time (ERT) published in customer-facing outage communications and OMS crew dispatch workflows.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; PUC Customer Communication Standards

description: Total duration of the power interruption at this customer service point in minutes, calculated from interruption start to end timestamp. Used directly in SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index) and CAIDI (Customer Average Interruption Duration Index) regulatory reporting.
The date and time when power supply was restored at this customer service point. Null if restoration has not yet occurred. Used in SAIDI/CAIDI duration calculations.

The date and time when power supply was interrupted at this customer service point. Used as the principal business event timestamp for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations and regulatory reporting.
reference: IEEE 1366 (Guide for Electric Power Distribution Reliability Indices); NERC TADS; PUC Reporting

description: The date and time when this affected customer association record was most recently modified, such as when restoration status was updated, notification was sent, or duration was recalculated.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Internal Data Governance Standards

description: Indicates whether this customer interruption is associated with a declared Major Event Day (MED) per IEEE 1366 criteria. Major event interruptions are excluded from standard SAIDI/SAIFI calculations and reported separately to regulators.
reference: IEEE 1366; PUC Major Event Reporting; NERC TADS

description: Indicates whether this interruption is classified as a momentary interruption (duration under 5 minutes per IEEE 1366 definition). Momentary interruptions are counted in MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) rather than SAIDI/SAIFI.

The communication channel used to deliver the outage notification to this customer (e.g., SMS text, email, Interactive Voice Response (IVR) call, mobile app push notification). Supports multi-channel outage communication tracking.
reference: PUC Customer Notification Rules; CRM Communication Standards

description: Indicates whether an outage notification has been sent to this customer for the associated outage event. Used to track compliance with PUC customer notification requirements.
reference: PUC Customer Notification Rules; State PUC Regulations

description: The date and time when the outage notification was sent to this customer. Used for regulatory compliance reporting on customer notification timeliness.
reference: PUC Customer Notification Rules; State PUC Regulations

description: External customer reference identifier as recorded in the Outage Management System (OMS), used for cross-system reconciliation between OMS and CIS records.

Classification of the outage event type affecting this customer. 'unplanned' = unexpected fault-driven interruption, 'planned' = scheduled maintenance window, 'momentary' = interruption under 5 minutes per IEEE 1366, 'major_event' = declared major event excluded from standard reliability indices.

The originally scheduled end time of the planned maintenance interruption for this customer service point. Populated only for planned outages. Compared against actual interruption_end_timestamp for schedule adherence reporting.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Ventyx Asset Suite; PUC Planned Outage Notification Rules

description: The originally scheduled start time of the planned maintenance interruption for this customer service point. Populated only for planned outages. Compared against actual interruption_start_timestamp for schedule adherence reporting.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Ventyx Asset Suite; PUC Planned Outage Notification Rules

description: Reason code indicating why this customer interruption is excluded from standard IEEE 1366 reliability index calculations. 'major_event' = IEEE 1366 MED exclusion, 'planned' = scheduled maintenance exclusion, 'momentary' = under 5-minute threshold, 'none' = included in standard indices.
reference: IEEE 1366; PUC Reliability Reporting; NERC TADS

description: The method by which power restoration was confirmed at this customer service point. 'ami_signal' = AMI meter power-on event, 'crew_verification' = field crew confirmation, 'customer_callback' = customer reported power restored, 'scada' = SCADA system indication.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Oracle Utilities MDM; SCADA Integration Standards

description: Indicates whether power restoration at this customer service point has been confirmed, either via AMI last-gasp/power-on signal, crew field verification, or customer callback. Distinguishes crew-reported restoration from confirmed restoration.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Oracle Utilities MDM AMI Integration; IEEE 1366

description: Current restoration lifecycle status for this customer service point. 'interrupted' = power out, 'restored' = power confirmed back, 'partial' = partial restoration, 'pending_confirmation' = crew reports restored but not yet confirmed, 'excluded' = excluded from reliability indices per IEEE 1366 major event criteria.
reference: IEEE 1366; GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; PUC Reporting

business_glossary_term: System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) Contribution (Minutes)
description: This customer's individual contribution to the SAIDI reliability index in customer-minutes. Equals the interruption duration in minutes for this customer record. Aggregated across all affected customers to compute system-level SAIDI per IEEE 1366.
reference: IEEE 1366; NERC TADS; PUC Reliability Reporting

business_glossary_term: System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI) Contribution
description: This customer's individual contribution to the SAIFI reliability index (value of 1 per sustained interruption, 0 for momentary). Aggregated across all affected customers to compute system-level SAIFI per IEEE 1366.
reference: IEEE 1366; NERC TADS; PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Code identifying the regulated service territory in which this customer service point is located. Used for jurisdictional reliability reporting to the applicable State Public Utility Commission (PUC).
reference: PUC Service Territory Definitions; SAP IS-U CIS

description: Indicates whether this customer interruption occurred during a declared storm or major weather event. Used for storm response performance reporting and potential major event day exclusion analysis.
reference: IEEE 1366; PUC Storm Reporting; NERC TADS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each customer notification record in the outage management system. Primary key for the customer_notification data product.
Reference to the customer account receiving this notification. Sourced from the Customer Information System (CIS) in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B.
reference: SAP IS-U CIS; Oracle Utilities CC&B

description: Foreign key linking to outage.affected_customer. Business justification: The customer_notification table records notifications sent to customers about outage events, but has no direct FK to the affected_customer association record. The affected_customer table is the author

Foreign key linking to interconnection.interconnection_agreement. Business justification: Notifications to DER customers must comply with interconnection agreement advance notice requirements, export curtailment notification timelines, and special notification provisions for critical DER f
Reference to the outage event that triggered this notification. Links the notification to the specific unplanned interruption or planned maintenance window in the Outage Management System (OMS).

Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: The customer_notification table has planned_outage_start_timestamp and planned_outage_end_timestamp as denormalized fields, but no FK to planned_outage_window. Notifications for planned outages must b

Foreign key linking to customer.premise. Business justification: Outage notifications are premise-specific (especially for planned outages and life support customers at specific service addresses). customer_notification has account_id but no premise FK; premise-lev
Reference to the specific service delivery point (premise) affected by the outage for which this notification was issued. Enables geographic and network-level analysis of notification coverage.
reference: SAP IS-U Device Management; Oracle CC&B Service Point

description: Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: The customer_notification table has no FK to storm_event despite notifications being a critical component of storm response operations. During storm events, mass notifications are issued and must be t
Timestamp when the customer acknowledged receipt of this notification. Null if no acknowledgment was received. Used for life-support welfare check compliance and two-way communication analytics.
reference: State PUC Life Support Customer Communication Rules

description: Number of hours of advance notice provided to the customer prior to a planned outage, calculated as the difference between notification sent timestamp and planned outage start timestamp. Used to verify compliance with state PUC minimum advance notice requirements (typically 48–72 hours).
reference: State PUC Planned Outage Advance Notice Requirements

description: Human-readable description of the outage cause as communicated to the customer in this notification (e.g., 'equipment failure on Main St. feeder', 'storm damage to transmission line'). Supports customer transparency and regulatory reporting.
reference: State PUC Customer Communication Standards; NERC FAC-002

description: The customer account number as recorded in the Customer Information System (CIS) — SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B — at the time of notification. Provides a direct cross-reference to the billing and service account for regulatory audit and dispute resolution.
reference: SAP IS-U CIS; Oracle Utilities CC&B

description: The specific contact address (email address, phone number, or device token) used for this notification delivery attempt, as resolved from the customer's preference profile. Stored for audit and delivery confirmation purposes. Classified as restricted PII.
reference: GDPR Article 4; TCPA; State PUC Customer Data Privacy Rules

description: Audit timestamp when this notification record was first created in the outage management system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.

Indicates whether the customer explicitly acknowledged receipt of this notification (e.g., via IVR keypress, SMS reply, or mobile app confirmation). True = acknowledged. Supports two-way communication tracking and life-support customer welfare checks.
reference: State PUC Life Support Customer Communication Rules

description: Total number of customers affected by the associated outage event at the time this notification was generated. Included in the notification for context and used in SAIFI (System Average Interruption Frequency Index) reporting.

Timestamp when delivery confirmation was received from the channel provider, confirming the notification reached the customer's device, inbox, or IVR endpoint. Null if delivery has not been confirmed or failed.

Current lifecycle state of the notification delivery attempt. pending: queued for dispatch; sent: transmitted to channel provider; delivered: confirmed receipt by customer device or inbox; failed: delivery attempt unsuccessful; bounced: message returned undeliverable; opted_out: customer has opted out of this channel.
reference: State PUC SLA Compliance Reporting; GDPR Article 5

description: The Estimated Time to Restore (ETR) communicated to the customer in this notification. Represents the utility's best estimate of when power will be restored to the affected service point. Critical for customer satisfaction and regulatory ETR accuracy reporting.
reference: State PUC ETR Reporting Requirements; IEEE 1366

description: Reason code for notification delivery failure when delivery_status is 'failed' or 'bounced'. Supports channel quality monitoring, contact data hygiene initiatives, and SLA root cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: invalid_contact|channel_unavailable|provider_error|customer_opted_out|number_disconnected|mailbox_full|unknown — promote to reference product]
reference: State PUC SLA Compliance Reporting; NAESB Retail Business Practices

description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) of the language in which the notification was composed and delivered. Supports multilingual customer communication compliance requirements mandated by state PUCs in diverse service territories.
reference: State PUC Language Access Requirements; ISO 639-1

description: Timestamp of the most recent delivery retry attempt for this notification. Null if no retries have been attempted. Used in conjunction with retry_count to assess delivery persistence and channel failure patterns.

Indicates whether the customer is registered as a life support or medical baseline customer at the time of this notification. True = life support customer. Triggers priority notification handling and regulatory reporting obligations under state PUC medical baseline rules.
reference: State PUC Medical Baseline and Life Support Customer Rules; CPUC Rule 14

description: Full text content of the notification message as delivered to the customer, including outage details, Estimated Time to Restore (ETR), and restoration instructions. Stored for regulatory audit and dispute resolution.
reference: State PUC Advance Notice Requirements; NAESB Retail Business Practices

description: Subject line or headline of the notification as delivered to the customer. Applicable primarily to email and mobile app push notifications. Stored for audit, regulatory review, and communication effectiveness analysis.
reference: State PUC Customer Communication Standards; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Identifier for the batch or campaign in which this notification was grouped and dispatched. Multiple customer notifications for the same outage event are typically dispatched in batches. Supports batch-level delivery performance analysis and storm response coordination.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS Integration; State PUC Storm Response Reporting

description: Communication channel through which the notification was dispatched to the customer. IVR: Interactive Voice Response automated call; SMS: text message; email: electronic mail; mobile_app: push notification via utility mobile application; web_portal: in-portal alert; postal_mail: physical letter for regulatory advance notice.
reference: State PUC Customer Communication Standards; NAESB Retail Business Practices

description: Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this notification record. Used in customer correspondence, regulatory audit trails, and SLA compliance tracking. Format: NTF- followed by 10-digit sequence.
reference: NERC CIP-014; State PUC Advance Notice Requirements

description: Priority level assigned to this notification, governing dispatch queue order and retry escalation logic. Critical priority is used for life-support customers and medical baseline accounts. Supports regulatory requirements for prioritized restoration communication.
reference: State PUC Medical Baseline and Life Support Customer Rules

description: Classification of the notification purpose. outage_alert: initial unplanned outage alert; etr_update: Estimated Time to Restore (ETR) revision; restoration_confirmation: power restored confirmation; planned_outage_notice: advance notice of scheduled maintenance; safety_alert: safety-related communication; demand_response_event: Demand Response (DR) program event notice.
reference: State PUC Advance Notice Requirements; NAESB Business Practices

description: External reference identifier assigned by the Outage Management System (OMS), specifically GE PowerOn ADMS, to this notification record. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational OMS source system.

Indicates whether the customer had opted out of this notification channel or notification type at the time of dispatch. True = customer opted out; notification may have been suppressed or sent via alternate channel per preference rules. Critical for TCPA and state PUC opt-out compliance.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act); State PUC Opt-Out Rules; GDPR Article 21

description: The timestamp of the outage event start as communicated to the customer in this notification. Provides the customer with context on when the interruption began. Used in SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations.

Classification of the outage event type associated with this notification. Distinguishes between unplanned interruptions, scheduled maintenance windows, storm-related events, equipment failures, and transmission faults. Supports SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric segmentation.
reference: IEEE 1366 Reliability Indices; NERC TPL Standards

description: Indicates whether this notification fulfills a specific regulatory advance notice obligation (e.g., state PUC-mandated 48-hour advance notice for planned outages). True = this notification is a regulatory compliance notice. Supports regulatory reporting and audit evidence.
reference: State PUC Planned Outage Advance Notice Requirements; NERC FAC-002

description: Number of delivery retry attempts made for this notification after an initial failure. Used to track delivery persistence, diagnose channel reliability issues, and support SLA compliance reporting.

The principal real-world event timestamp when the notification was dispatched from the utility's outage communication platform to the delivery channel provider. Used as the primary business event time for SLA measurement and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: State PUC Advance Notice Requirements; NERC FAC-002

description: Indicates whether this notification was dispatched within the required Service Level Agreement (SLA) target time. True = SLA met; False = SLA breached. Supports regulatory SLA compliance reporting to state PUCs and internal performance dashboards.
reference: State PUC Customer Communication SLA Requirements

description: The Service Level Agreement (SLA) target time in minutes within which this notification type must be dispatched following the triggering outage event. Defined by state PUC regulations and internal communication standards. Used as the benchmark for SLA compliance measurement.
reference: State PUC Customer Communication SLA Requirements

description: Identifies the operational system of record that originated this notification record. Primarily GE PowerOn ADMS for OMS-driven notifications, but may include SAP IS-U, Oracle CC&B for billing-related outage notices, or manual OMS entries during storm response.
reference: Enterprise Data Lineage Standards; SOX Section 404

description: Code identifying the utility's service territory or operating division within which the affected customer and service point are located. Supports territory-level SLA compliance reporting to individual state PUCs and regional storm response performance analysis.
reference: State PUC Service Territory Definitions; NERC Reliability Standards

description: Audit timestamp of the most recent modification to this notification record, such as a delivery status update or retry attempt. Supports change tracking and SLA audit.
Unique surrogate identifier for each inbound customer trouble call or AMI-generated outage report record in the Outage Management System (OMS). Primary key for this entity.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Call Management

description: Reference to the utility customer account associated with the caller or AMI device that originated this outage report. Links to the Customer Information System (CIS) account record.
reference: SAP IS-U CIS; Oracle Utilities CC&B — Account Management

description: Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Trouble calls from DER customers may indicate system malfunction, islanding issues, or anti-islanding protection failures requiring DER system reference for proper diagnosis and dispatch prioritizatio

Identifier of the distribution substation serving the reported outage location, resolved from the GIS network model. Used for geographic grouping of calls, outage event scoping, and storm response coordination.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network — Substation Asset Model; GE PowerOn ADMS

description: Reference to the original (first-received) outage call record of which this call is a duplicate. Populated only when is_duplicate = True. Enables deduplication lineage tracing in OMS call clustering.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Call Clustering

description: Reference to the parent outage event record once this call has been matched or clustered to a confirmed outage event in the OMS. Null until the call is correlated to an event.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Event Management

description: Identifier of the distribution circuit (feeder) serving the reported outage location, resolved from the GIS network model based on the service address or premise. Used for OMS call clustering by circuit and outage event correlation.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network — Distribution Network Model; GE PowerOn ADMS — ADMS (Advanced Distribution Management System) Circuit Management

description: Reference to the smart meter or AMI endpoint that generated an automated outage ping or last-gasp signal, if the call source is AMI-initiated rather than a human caller.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM — Meter Data Management; AMI Infrastructure

description: Reference to the utility service premise (physical delivery point) associated with the reported outage location. Links to the GIS/network model for spatial outage clustering and distribution circuit identification.
reference: SAP IS-U — Device Management; Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; GE PowerOn ADMS

description: Reference to the declared storm or major weather event record associated with this outage call, when weather_event_flag = True. Links to the storm event management entity for storm response coordination and MED exclusion processing.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Major Event Day (MED); GE PowerOn ADMS — Storm Management; State PUC Storm Reporting

description: Number of customers the caller reports as being affected by the outage (e.g., 'my whole street is out'). This is a caller-provided estimate, not a system-confirmed count. Used as an early indicator for outage scope assessment and crew dispatch prioritization.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Outage Scope Assessment; IEEE 1366

description: Unique identifier of the AMI endpoint (smart meter) that generated an automated last-gasp outage notification or power-off ping. Populated only when call_source_type = AMI_PING. Used to correlate AMI-detected outages with customer account and network topology.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM — AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure) Device Management; ANSI C12.19

description: Type of automated signal received from the AMI endpoint that triggered this outage report. LAST_GASP = meter-detected power loss notification; POWER_RESTORE = meter-detected power restoration; TAMPER = tamper event; BLINK = momentary interruption blink count; POWER_QUALITY = voltage sag/swell event. Populated only when call_source_type = AMI_PING.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM — AMI Signal Processing; IEEE 1159 Power Quality

description: Current lifecycle status of the outage call record within the OMS workflow. RECEIVED = call logged, not yet processed; PENDING_MATCH = awaiting correlation to an outage event; MATCHED = successfully linked to an outage event; DUPLICATE = identified as a duplicate of an existing call or event; CLOSED = call resolved and closed; CANCELLED = call withdrawn or invalid.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Call Management Workflow

description: Full name of the individual who placed the trouble call, as provided at time of call. May differ from the account holder name if a third party is reporting on behalf of the customer. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR/CCPA.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Oracle Utilities CC&B — Call Management

description: Phone number provided by or captured from the caller at time of call receipt. Used for call-back confirmation and customer notification of restoration. Classified as restricted PII per GDPR/CCPA.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; Oracle Utilities CC&B — Call Management

description: Date and time at which this outage call record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer ingestion timestamp). Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and regulatory record-keeping. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; NERC Reliability Reporting Audit Trail; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Classification of the customer associated with this outage call by service type. Used for restoration prioritization, regulatory reporting, and SAIDI/SAIFI index segmentation per IEEE 1366 and PUC requirements.
reference: IEEE 1366; State PUC Tariff Classification; SAP IS-U — Customer Classification

description: Indicates the data quality status of this outage call record as assessed during Silver Layer ingestion and validation. VALID = passed all validation rules; SUSPECT = passed ingestion but has anomalies requiring review; INVALID = failed critical validation; PENDING_REVIEW = awaiting manual data quality review.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Quality Standards; Oracle Utilities MDM — VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing)

description: Standardized code indicating the final outcome or resolution action taken on this call (e.g., EVENT_CREATED, ADDED_TO_EVENT, DUPLICATE_CLOSED, NO_OUTAGE_FOUND, REFERRED_TO_CREW, CUSTOMER_CALLBACK_SCHEDULED). Sourced from OMS disposition reference table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EVENT_CREATED|ADDED_TO_EVENT|DUPLICATE_CLOSED|NO_OUTAGE_FOUND|REFERRED_TO_CREW|CUSTOMER_CALLBACK_SCHEDULED|INVALID — promote to reference product]
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Call Disposition; Oracle Utilities CC&B

description: Duration of the customer call in seconds from answer to disconnect, for calls received via IVR or live agent channels. Used for call center operational reporting, SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance, and customer experience analytics.
reference: Oracle Utilities CC&B — Call Center Operations; State PUC Customer Service Standards

description: Estimated date and time of power restoration communicated to the customer at the time of call handling or subsequent notification. Sourced from OMS restoration forecasting. Used for customer expectation management and SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Restoration Forecasting; State PUC Customer Service Standards

description: Indicates whether the caller reported a safety hazard associated with the outage (e.g., downed power line, sparking equipment, fire, flooding near electrical equipment). True = hazard reported; False = no hazard reported. Triggers priority escalation in OMS crew dispatch and public safety notification workflows.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.269 — Electric Power Generation, Transmission, and Distribution; GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Priority Escalation

description: Specific type of safety hazard reported by the caller when hazard_indicator = True. Used to determine emergency response protocol, crew safety briefing requirements, and coordination with emergency services (fire, police). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DOWNED_LINE|SPARKING|FIRE|FLOODING|EQUIPMENT_DAMAGE|TREE_ON_LINE|VEHICLE_ACCIDENT|OTHER — promote to reference product]
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.269; NERC CIP; GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Hazard Management

description: Indicates whether this call has been identified as a duplicate report of an outage already captured by a prior call or AMI notification for the same event and location. True = duplicate; False = unique first report. Used by OMS call clustering logic to avoid double-counting in reliability metrics.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Call Clustering; IEEE 1366

description: Unique session identifier assigned by the Interactive Voice Response (IVR) system for calls received through the automated phone channel. Used for call traceability, IVR system reconciliation, and customer experience analytics. Populated only when call_source_type = IVR.
reference: Oracle Utilities CC&B — IVR (Interactive Voice Response) Integration; SAP IS-U CIS

description: Date and time at which this call was successfully matched or correlated to a confirmed outage event in the OMS. Populated when call_status transitions to MATCHED. Used for OMS processing latency analysis and reliability reporting timelines. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Event Correlation

description: Indicates whether the affected customer account is registered on the utility's medical baseline or life-support program, requiring priority restoration and proactive notification per PUC regulations. True = medical baseline customer; False = standard customer. Classified as confidential due to sensitivity of health-related program enrollment.
reference: State PUC Medical Baseline Program Rules; NERC EOP Standards; Oracle Utilities CC&B — Customer Program Management

description: Indicates whether an outage acknowledgement or estimated restoration time (ERT) notification has been sent to the customer associated with this call. True = notification sent; False = not yet sent. Supports PUC customer notification compliance tracking.
reference: State PUC Customer Notification Requirements; GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Customer Notification

description: Date and time at which the outage acknowledgement or estimated restoration time (ERT) notification was dispatched to the customer. Populated when notification_sent_flag = True. Used for PUC notification compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: State PUC Customer Notification Requirements; GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Notification Management

business_glossary_term: Outage Management System (OMS) Call Cluster ID
description: Identifier of the OMS call cluster to which this call has been assigned during the outage prediction and event correlation process. Multiple calls from the same geographic area or circuit are grouped into a cluster to predict the probable outage location and initiate crew dispatch before a formal event is confirmed.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS (Outage Management System) Call Clustering and Outage Prediction

description: Date and time the caller reports the outage began, as stated by the customer or detected by the AMI device. May differ from the OMS-confirmed outage start time. Used as an input to outage event timeline reconstruction and SAIDI/CAIDI calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Reliability Indices; GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Event Timeline

description: Restoration priority level assigned to this outage call based on hazard indicators, medical baseline status, customer class, and outage scope. CRITICAL = life-safety or critical infrastructure; HIGH = medical baseline or large commercial/industrial; MEDIUM = standard residential or small commercial; LOW = non-urgent or duplicate. Drives OMS crew dispatch sequencing.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Restoration Prioritization; State PUC Service Reliability Rules

description: Date and time at which the outage call or AMI-generated outage report was received by the utility system. This is the principal business event timestamp used for OMS call clustering, outage prediction, and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability index calculations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Guide for Electric Power Distribution Reliability Indices; NERC Reliability Standards

description: Externally visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to this trouble call at receipt, used for customer-facing acknowledgement (e.g., IVR confirmation code, agent ticket number, web portal confirmation). Sourced from the CIS or OMS call intake module.
reference: SAP IS-U; Oracle Utilities CC&B — Call Management

description: Standardized code representing the symptom or problem reported by the caller or detected by the AMI system (e.g., FULL_OUTAGE, PARTIAL_OUTAGE, FLICKERING, LOW_VOLTAGE, DOWNED_LINE, BURNING_SMELL). Sourced from the OMS symptom code reference table. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FULL_OUTAGE|PARTIAL_OUTAGE|FLICKERING|LOW_VOLTAGE|DOWNED_LINE|BURNING_SMELL|SPARKING|BUZZING — promote to reference product]
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Symptom Codes; IEEE 1366

description: Free-text description of the outage symptom or problem as reported by the caller or transcribed by the call center agent. Supplements the structured symptom code with verbatim customer language for OMS analysis and crew dispatch context.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Call Notes; Oracle Utilities CC&B — Call Management

description: Street address of the service location reported as experiencing the outage, as provided by the caller or resolved from the account record. Used for geographic clustering in the OMS and crew dispatch routing.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Esri ArcGIS Utility Network — Geospatial Asset Location; GE PowerOn ADMS

description: Code identifying the utility's regulated service territory or operating division in which the reported outage is located. Used for regulatory reporting segmentation to State PUCs and NERC reliability reporting.
reference: State PUC Service Territory Definitions; NERC Reliability Reporting; SAP IS-U — Service Territory

description: Original call or ticket identifier assigned by the source operational system (e.g., OMS call number from GE PowerOn ADMS, CIS interaction ID from SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer data product and the system of record.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Call Management; SAP IS-U; Oracle Utilities CC&B

description: Channel or mechanism through which the outage report was received. IVR = Interactive Voice Response automated phone system; AGENT = live call center agent; AMI_PING = Advanced Metering Infrastructure last-gasp or outage notification; MOBILE_APP = utility mobile application; WEB_PORTAL = online self-service portal; FIELD_CREW = field crew-initiated report.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Call Intake; AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure) Standards

description: Date and time at which this outage call record was last modified in the data platform, reflecting any status updates, event matching, or data corrections applied during the call lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; NERC Reliability Reporting Audit Trail; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Indicates whether the outage reported in this call is associated with a declared weather event (storm, hurricane, ice storm, heat wave, etc.). business_glossary_term: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) 1366 Interruption Category
description: Classification of the outage cause under IEEE Standard 1366 for electric power distribution reliability indices. Determines how interruptions associated with this cause are counted in SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI calculations.

Indicates whether outage events attributed to this cause are eligible for insurance claim submission (e.g., storm damage, third-party damage). Supports financial recovery tracking and CAPEX/OPEX classification.

Indicates whether the outage cause is considered controllable by the utility (i.e., preventable through operational or maintenance actions). Used in reliability improvement program targeting and PUC performance benchmarking.

Indicates whether outage events with this cause require submission to the U.S. Department of Energy via the OE-417 Electric Emergency Incident and Disturbance Report. True = DOE OE-417 filing required.

Indicates whether outage events attributed to this cause qualify for exclusion from SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI baseline calculations as a Major Event Day (MED) under IEEE 1366. True = MED-eligible exclusion; False = included in standard reliability indices.

Indicates whether this cause code is associated with momentary interruptions (duration less than 5 minutes per IEEE 1366). Momentary events are tracked separately in MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) calculations.

business_glossary_term: North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) Reportable Flag
description: Indicates whether outage events attributed to this cause trigger mandatory NERC event reporting obligations (e.g., EOP-004 threshold events). True = NERC reporting required; False = not required.

Indicates whether this cause code applies to planned (scheduled) outages rather than unplanned interruptions. Planned outages are excluded from SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculations per IEEE 1366. True = planned; False = unplanned.

Indicates whether outage events with this cause must be reported to the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) under applicable regulatory reporting requirements. True = mandatory PUC notification required; False = not required.
reference: State PUC Reporting Requirements; NERC EOP-004

description: Indicates whether the outage cause is classified as weather-related. Drives storm response analytics, MED determination, and insurance/force-majeure reporting. Distinct from the cause_category field as some sub-causes may be weather-adjacent.

business_glossary_term: North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) Cause Category
description: NERC-defined top-level cause category label corresponding to the nerc_cause_code. Supports direct mapping to NERC event reporting taxonomy without requiring a separate lookup. Examples: 'Electrical', 'Environmental', 'Human Error', 'Other'.

business_glossary_term: North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) Cause Code

description: The native cause code identifier as configured in the GE PowerOn ADMS/OMS system. Used for direct system integration, data lineage tracing, and reconciliation between the silver layer and the source OMS.

The cause code as defined in the applicable state Public Utility Commission (PUC) reporting taxonomy. Required for annual reliability reports and service quality filings. May differ from internal cause_code.

Free-text field capturing specific regulatory reporting instructions, exceptions, or conditions associated with this cause code. Used by compliance teams to document PUC, NERC, or DOE reporting nuances for this cause.
reference: NERC EOP-004; State PUC Reporting Requirements; DOE OE-417

description: Identifies which reliability performance indices (SAIDI, SAIFI, CAIDI, MAIFI) are impacted by outage events attributed to this cause. Drives automated index calculation logic in the reliability analytics platform.

business_glossary_term: System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) Weight Factor
description: Numeric weighting factor applied to SAIDI calculations for outages of this cause type. Allows regulatory-mandated cause-specific weighting adjustments in reliability index computations. Default is 1.0 (no adjustment).
Numeric value controlling the display sequence of cause codes in OMS dropdown lists, regulatory report templates, and analytics dashboards. Lower values appear first.
