description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this cause code was sourced or synchronized (e.g., GE PowerOn ADMS, SAP IS-U). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks silver layer.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cause code record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance.

description: Method by which the fault was isolated from the network: automatic (protective device operated automatically), manual (field crew operated device manually), remote (SCADA/ADMS remote switching), or flisr_automated (ADMS FLISR automation executed switching sequence).
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS FLISR Module; IEEE C37; NERC FAC-002

description: Indicates whether this outage event meets NERC reporting thresholds (e.g., EOP-004 disturbance reporting criteria based on MW loss, customer count, or duration). True = event must be reported to NERC/DOE via OE-417 or equivalent.
reference: NERC EOP-004 Disturbance Reporting; DOE OE-417 Electric Emergency Incident Reporting

description: Indicates whether automated customer outage notifications (IVR, SMS, email, or app push) have been sent for this outage event. True = at least one notification batch dispatched. Used for regulatory compliance tracking of customer communication requirements.
reference: State PUC Customer Notification Requirements; Oracle CC&B Notification Module

description: Timestamp when the first customer outage notification was dispatched for this event. Used to measure notification latency from detection and to demonstrate compliance with PUC customer communication time requirements.
reference: State PUC Customer Notification Requirements; Oracle CC&B

description: The externally-known event identifier assigned by the Outage Management System (GE PowerOn ADMS). This is the operational business identifier used by field crews, dispatchers, and customer service representatives to reference the outage event across systems.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — Outage Event; NAESB Business Practice Standards

description: The cause code predicted by the OMS predictive model or ADMS fault analysis engine at the time of initial detection, before field crew investigation. Compared against final cause_code for model accuracy tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|equipment_failure|animal_contact|vegetation|human_error|unknown — promote to reference product]
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS Predictive Analytics; IEEE 1366-2012

description: The number of customers predicted to be affected by the outage at the time of initial detection, as estimated by the OMS/ADMS predictive model. Compared against actual customers_affected_count for model accuracy and ETR calibration.

Confidence score (0.00–100.00) assigned by the predictive model or ADMS fault analysis engine to the initial outage prediction, including predicted cause and affected scope. Used to assess model reliability and calibrate automated dispatch thresholds.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS Predictive Analytics Module

description: Restoration priority classification assigned to the outage event, used to sequence crew dispatch and switching operations. Critical: hospitals, emergency services, critical infrastructure; High: large commercial/industrial, high customer count; Medium: standard residential; Low: single-customer or non-critical.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS Restoration Prioritization; State PUC Critical Facility Requirements; NERC EOP-011

business_glossary_term: State Public Utility Commission (PUC) Reportable Event Flag
description: Indicates whether this outage event meets the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) threshold for mandatory individual event reporting (e.g., exceeds customer count or duration thresholds defined in the utility's tariff or service quality rules).
reference: State PUC Service Quality and Reliability Rules; NERC FAC-002

description: The primary symptom reported by the first customer caller or detected by the AMI/SCADA system at the time of initial report. Used for triage, cause hypothesis, and crew dispatch guidance.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS; Oracle CC&B Trouble Call Management

business_glossary_term: System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) Contribution in Minutes
description: This event's contribution to the System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI), expressed in customer-minutes per total customers served. Calculated as customer_minutes_interrupted divided by total customers served in the reporting period. Stored at event level for regulatory period aggregation.
reference: IEEE 1366-2012 SAIDI Definition; State PUC Annual Reliability Report

business_glossary_term: System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI) Contribution
description: This event's contribution to the System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI), expressed as customer interruptions per total customers served. Calculated as customers_affected_count divided by total customers served. Stored at event level for regulatory period aggregation.
reference: IEEE 1366-2012 SAIFI Definition; State PUC Annual Reliability Report

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the outage event record. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
Unique surrogate identifier for a single customer or premise-level service interruption record. Primary key for this entity.
reference: IEEE 1366 - Guide for Electric Power Distribution Reliability Indices

description: Reference to the customer account associated with the interrupted service point at the time of the interruption. Supports customer-facing outage communication and regulatory customer impact reporting.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA; Oracle Utilities CC&B Customer Account

description: The AMI meter identifier associated with the service point at the time of the interruption. Used to correlate last-gasp and first-breath AMI signals from the MDM system for automated interruption detection and restoration confirmation.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM; AMI/Advanced Metering Infrastructure Standards; IEEE 1366

description: Foreign key linking to billing.billing_service_agreement. Business justification: Individual customer interruptions require service agreement context to apply correct credit calculation rules based on rate schedule, service class, and contractual SLA terms. Enables automated credit
FK to outage.cause
Reference to the field crew dispatch record associated with the restoration of this interruption. Links to workforce management for crew response time analysis and restoration efficiency reporting.
reference: Ventyx Asset Suite Work Management; GE PowerOn ADMS OMS

description: Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Customer interruptions at DER-equipped service points require DER system reference for islanding analysis, restoration sequencing, and regulatory reporting of DER performance during grid disturbances
Reference to the substation serving the affected service point at the time of the interruption. Supports substation-level reliability performance reporting and outage root cause analysis.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; GE PowerOn ADMS; NERC FAC-002

description: Reference to the parent outage event that caused this customer-level interruption. Links the granular interruption record to the broader outage event for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI roll-up calculations.
reference: IEEE 1366; GE PowerOn ADMS OMS Event Record

description: Reference to the distribution feeder circuit that serves the affected service point. Supports feeder-level reliability analysis, SAIDI/SAIFI benchmarking by circuit, and GIS-based outage mapping.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; GE PowerOn ADMS; IEEE 1366

description: Reference to the planned maintenance window associated with this interruption, when applicable. Null for unplanned outages. Links to the planned outage schedule for customer notification and regulatory reporting.
reference: NERC FAC-002; Ventyx Asset Suite Work Management

description: Foreign key linking to metering.meter_point. Business justification: Interruptions must reference the meter_point (AMI collection point) directly for interval data gap analysis, automated outage detection via AMI, and SAIDI/SAIFI calculations at the meter_point level.
Reference to the switching order executed to isolate or restore the affected circuit segment. Links the customer-level interruption to the operational switching workflow in the ADMS/OMS.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS Switching Management; NERC FAC-001

description: Reference to the specific service delivery point (meter point or premise connection) that experienced the interruption. The fundamental unit of interruption tracking per IEEE 1366.
reference: IEEE 1366; SAP IS-U Device Management; Oracle CC&B Service Point

description: FK to distribution.service_territory

FK to outage.planned_outage_window.planned_outage_window_id — Customer interruptions from planned maintenance link to the planned outage window. Required for distinguishing planned vs unplanned interruptions in reliability reporting.
Date and time when this interruption record was first created in the system, either by automated OMS event processing or manual dispatcher entry. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; NERC CIP-007; GE PowerOn ADMS OMS

description: Specific category of critical care designation for the affected customer. Used to prioritize restoration sequencing and determine notification requirements. Only populated when is_critical_care is true.
reference: State PUC Medical Baseline Rules; CPUC Rule 14; HIPAA (for health-related designations)

description: Classification of the affected customer by service class at the time of the interruption. Used for segmented SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reporting by customer class as required by state PUC reliability standards and NERC reporting.
reference: IEEE 1366; State PUC Reliability Reporting; NERC Reliability Standards

description: Number of customers affected by the parent outage event at the time this interruption record was created. Denormalized from the outage event for regulatory reporting convenience. Used in SAIFI numerator calculations and NERC/PUC customer impact reporting.
reference: IEEE 1366; NERC EOP-004; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Indicates the data quality status of this interruption record as assessed during Silver layer validation. 'validated' indicates all timestamps and durations are confirmed; 'estimated' indicates one or more fields are estimated; 'suspect' indicates anomalies detected; 'corrected' indicates the record has been manually revised post-initial capture.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM VEE (Validation, Estimation, and Editing); Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Quality Rules

description: Total duration of the service interruption in whole minutes, calculated as the difference between interruption_end_timestamp and interruption_start_timestamp. Stored as a business field to support SAIDI and CAIDI reliability index calculations without requiring real-time computation. Null if interruption is still active.
reference: IEEE 1366; NERC Reliability Reporting; State PUC Reliability Standards

description: Date and time when the customer's service was restored, recorded to millisecond precision. Null if the interruption is still active. Used as the end point for duration calculations and CAIDI index computation per IEEE 1366.
reference: IEEE 1366; GE PowerOn ADMS; Oracle Utilities CC&B

description: Estimated date and time of service restoration communicated to the customer and used for crew scheduling. Distinct from interruption_end_timestamp (actual restoration). Supports customer communication accuracy tracking and ETR (Estimated Time to Restore) performance measurement.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS ETR; State PUC Customer Notification Rules; Oracle Utilities CC&B

description: Current lifecycle state of the interruption record. 'active' indicates the customer is currently without power; 'restored' indicates service has been re-energized; 'cancelled' indicates the interruption was logged in error; 'pending_confirmation' indicates restoration has been initiated but field confirmation is outstanding.

Classification of the interruption as planned (scheduled maintenance), unplanned (forced outage), or momentary (duration less than 5 minutes per IEEE 1366 definition). Drives separate SAIDI/SAIFI/MAIFI index tracking and regulatory reporting segmentation.
reference: IEEE 1366 Section 3; NERC Reliability Reporting; State PUC Reliability Standards

description: Indicates whether the affected customer or premise is designated as a critical care account (medical baseline, life support equipment, or other medically vulnerable designation). Critical care customers receive priority restoration and mandatory advance notification under state PUC rules.
reference: State PUC Medical Baseline Rules; CPUC Rule 14; NERC EOP-011

description: Date and time when this interruption record was most recently modified, including status updates, restoration confirmation, or cause code revisions. Supports change tracking and Silver layer incremental processing.
reference: SOX Section 404; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Change Data Capture

description: Indicates whether this interruption has been formally excluded from the standard SAIDI/SAIFI reliability index calculations under the IEEE 1366 Major Event Day beta method. Distinct from storm_event_flag — this is the formal regulatory exclusion determination.
reference: IEEE 1366 Section 4.2 Major Event Day Beta Method; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Indicates whether this interruption qualifies as a momentary interruption (duration less than 5 minutes per IEEE 1366 definition). Momentary interruptions are tracked separately for MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) calculations.
reference: IEEE 1366 Section 3 Momentary Interruption Definition; State PUC MAIFI Reporting

description: Communication channel used to deliver the outage notification to the affected customer. Supports multi-channel notification analytics and customer preference compliance reporting.
reference: State PUC Customer Notification Rules; Oracle Utilities CC&B; SAP IS-U

description: Indicates whether the customer has opted out of outage notifications for this service point. When true, notification_sent will be false by design. Supports customer preference management and regulatory opt-out compliance.
reference: State PUC Customer Communication Rules; TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act)

description: Indicates whether an outage notification has been sent to the affected customer for this interruption. Supports regulatory compliance tracking for customer notification requirements mandated by state PUCs.
reference: State PUC Customer Notification Rules; Oracle Utilities CC&B Notification

description: Date and time when the outage notification was dispatched to the customer. Used to measure notification lead time relative to interruption start and to demonstrate regulatory compliance with advance notice requirements for planned outages.
reference: State PUC Customer Notification Rules; NAESB Business Practices

description: The externally-known event number assigned by the Outage Management System (GE PowerOn ADMS) to the parent outage event. Serves as the cross-system integration key for reconciliation between OMS, CIS, and MDM systems.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; SAP IS-U; Oracle Utilities CC&B

description: Type of protective device that operated to isolate the fault causing this interruption. Supports equipment performance analysis, recloser operation tracking, and fuse-saving vs. trip-and-reclose strategy evaluation.
reference: IEEE 1366; GE PowerOn ADMS; NERC PRC Standards

description: Externally visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to this interruption record, used in customer communications, regulatory filings, and OMS integration. Format: INT-YYYY-NNNNNNNN.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Oracle Utilities CC&B; NERC Reliability Reporting

description: Indicates whether this interruption must be included in regulatory reliability reports submitted to NERC, FERC, or state PUCs. Interruptions below minimum duration thresholds or excluded under MED rules may be flagged as non-reportable.
reference: NERC EOP-004; FERC Form 1; State PUC Annual Reliability Reports; IEEE 1366

description: Method by which service restoration was confirmed. AMI ping uses Advanced Metering Infrastructure last-gasp/first-breath signals; field_crew indicates on-site verification; customer_callback indicates customer-reported restoration; scada indicates SCADA system re-energization signal; estimated indicates restoration assumed based on switching operations.
reference: IEEE 1366; Oracle Utilities MDM; GE PowerOn ADMS; NERC EOP-004

description: Indicates whether service restoration has been confirmed for this interruption, either through AMI meter ping, field crew confirmation, or customer callback. Distinguishes between estimated restoration and verified restoration for CAIDI accuracy.
reference: IEEE 1366; GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; Oracle Utilities MDM AMI

description: Method used to restore service to the affected customer or premise. Supports operational analysis of restoration efficiency and crew dispatch effectiveness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: switching|repair|bypass|auto_reclosure|manual_transfer|temporary_generation — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS FLISR; IEEE 1366; NERC EOP-004

business_glossary_term: System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) Contribution (Minutes)
description: This interruption's individual contribution to the SAIDI reliability index, expressed in customer-minutes divided by total customers served. Stored as a pre-computed business field to support regulatory reporting aggregation without requiring full recalculation. Null if interruption is active or excluded from SAIDI.
reference: IEEE 1366 SAIDI Definition; State PUC Reliability Reporting; NERC Reliability Standards

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this interruption record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking across OMS, CIS, and MDM source systems.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; GE PowerOn ADMS; Oracle Utilities CC&B; SAP IS-U

description: Date and time when the customer's service interruption began, recorded to millisecond precision. This is the principal business event timestamp used as the start point for duration calculations and SAIDI/SAIFI index computation per IEEE 1366.
reference: IEEE 1366; NERC EOP-004; GE PowerOn ADMS

description: Indicates whether this interruption occurred during a declared major event or storm. When true, the interruption may be excluded from IEEE 1366 Major Event Day (MED) calculations per the beta method, affecting SAIDI/SAIFI index reporting.
reference: IEEE 1366 Major Event Day (MED) Beta Method; State PUC Storm Reporting Rules

description: FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Every customer-level interruption must link to its parent outage event. This is the foundational relationship for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculation — without it, reliability indices cannot be computed. Da

Voltage classification of the circuit segment at which the interruption originated. Used to segment reliability reporting by voltage class and to identify transmission vs. distribution-level outage impacts for FERC and state PUC reporting.
reference: NERC Reliability Standards; FERC Form 1; IEEE 1366; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Prevailing weather condition at the time and location of the interruption. Used for weather-normalized reliability analysis, storm response performance reporting, and climate resilience planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|wind|ice|snow|lightning|flood|extreme_heat|none — promote to reference product if additional values needed]
reference: IEEE 1366; State PUC Storm Reporting; NERC EOP-004

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the mutual aid request record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3 (Transmission System Planning Performance Requirements); IEEE 1366

description: Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Mutual aid requests are scoped to the requesting utility's control area, which defines the geographic and operational boundary of the emergency. NERC ESOP and regional mutual aid agreements are organi
Reference to the outage event that triggered this mutual aid request. Links the aid request to the specific storm, major outage, or emergency event driving the need for external resources.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS event record; NERC EOP-005-3

description: FK to outage.storm_event.storm_event_id — Mutual aid requests are issued in context of a storm event. Must link to parent storm for coordination and cost tracking.

Foreign key linking to forecast.weather_input. Business justification: Mutual aid deployment decisions require weather forecast data to determine crew mobilization timing, staging locations, and resource quantities. Weather severity forecasts justify mutual aid requests
Date and time when the responding utility formally acknowledged receipt of the mutual aid request. Used to measure response time SLA compliance.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; ESOP response time standards

description: Actual total cost in US dollars incurred for the mutual aid deployment upon demobilization, as submitted by the responding utility for reimbursement. Populated after demobilization and cost reconciliation.
reference: FERC cost recovery rules; EEI Mutual Assistance Program; SAP S/4HANA AP/AR settlement

description: Number of customers without power at the time the mutual aid request was issued. Provides context for the scale of the outage event and justifies the level of external resource request.
reference: IEEE 1366; NERC EOP-005-3; State PUC reporting requirements

description: Actual date on which the responding utility's crews arrived at the staging area or work location within the requesting utility's service territory and became available for restoration work.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; ESOP staging and logistics

description: Date and time when the responding utility formally committed to providing the requested resources, transitioning the request from acknowledged to committed status.

Indicates whether the requesting utility has agreed to reimburse the responding utility for costs incurred during the mutual aid deployment. True = reimbursable; False = non-reimbursable (goodwill aid). Drives cost tracking and invoice generation workflows.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; EEI Mutual Assistance Program cost reimbursement guidelines; FERC cost recovery rules

description: Date and time when this mutual aid request record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.
reference: SOX Section 404; NERC CIP-007; internal data governance policy

description: Number of crew units actually committed by the responding utility. May be less than crew_count_requested if the responding utility has limited available resources.
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP resource commitment

description: Actual number of crew units that physically arrived and performed restoration work in the requesting utility's territory. May differ from committed count due to last-minute changes or attrition during transit.
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP deployment tracking

description: Number of crew units (typically crew trucks or work teams) requested from the responding utility. A crew unit typically consists of a truck and associated personnel per ESOP standards.
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP resource quantification

description: Classification of crew type actually committed by the responding utility. May differ from crew_type_requested if the responding utility can only provide a partial or alternative resource match. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: line_crew|tree_crew|damage_assessment|substation|transmission|logistics — promote to reference product]
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP crew classification standards

description: Classification of crew type requested by the requesting utility (e.g., line crew, tree trimming crew, damage assessment crew, substation crew, transmission crew, logistics crew). Drives resource matching in ESOP coordination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: line_crew|tree_crew|damage_assessment|substation|transmission|logistics — promote to reference product]
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP crew classification standards

description: Actual date on which the responding utility's crews completed their assignment and were released to return to their home utility. Marks the end of active mutual aid deployment.
Number of equipment units of the specified type actually committed by the responding utility.
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP resource commitment

description: Number of equipment units of the specified type requested from the responding utility.
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP resource quantification

description: Type of specialized equipment requested alongside crews, such as bucket trucks, digger derricks, cable pullers, generators, or mobile command units. Supplements crew type in resource matching.
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP equipment classification

business_glossary_term: ESOP (Emergency Support Operations Plan) Reference Number
description: Reference number of the Emergency Support Operations Plan (ESOP) or mutual aid agreement under which this request is issued. Ties the request to the governing bilateral or regional aid compact.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; Edison Electric Institute (EEI) Mutual Assistance Program

description: Estimated total cost in US dollars for the mutual aid deployment, including labor, equipment, lodging, and travel expenses. Used for budget planning and pre-authorization of reimbursable aid.
reference: FERC cost recovery rules; EEI Mutual Assistance Program; SAP S/4HANA cost management

description: Classification of the weather or emergency event that triggered the mutual aid request. Used for post-event analysis, NERC regulatory reporting, and storm response benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hurricane|ice_storm|tornado|wildfire|flood|earthquake|major_outage|other — promote to reference product]
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; FEMA National Incident Management System (NIMS); IEEE 1366

description: Current status of the cost reimbursement invoice associated with this mutual aid request. Tracks the financial settlement lifecycle from invoice submission through payment or dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AR/AP; FERC cost recovery; EEI Mutual Assistance Program

description: Date and time when this mutual aid request record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and Silver Layer incremental processing.
reference: SOX Section 404; NERC CIP-007; internal data governance policy

description: Planned or actual date on which the committed crews and resources depart from the responding utility and begin travel to the requesting utility's service territory.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; EEI Mutual Assistance Program; ESOP mobilization procedures

business_glossary_term: NERC (North American Electric Reliability Corporation) Reportable Flag
description: Indicates whether this mutual aid request must be reported to NERC under EOP-005-3 or other applicable reliability standards. True = reportable event requiring NERC submission.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; NERC Rules of Procedure Section 1600

business_glossary_term: NERC (North American Electric Reliability Corporation) Submission Timestamp
description: Date and time when this mutual aid event was submitted to NERC for regulatory reporting. Null if nerc_reportable_flag is False or submission is pending.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; NERC Rules of Procedure Section 1600

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or coordination details associated with the mutual aid request. May include staging instructions, safety briefing requirements, or special equipment needs.

Reference identifier from the Outage Management System (GE PowerOn ADMS) for the outage event associated with this mutual aid request. Enables traceability between the aid request and the OMS event record.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS integration; NERC EOP-005-3

description: Priority classification of the mutual aid request, reflecting the severity of the outage event and urgency of resource need. Drives dispatch sequencing when multiple requests compete for limited responding utility resources.

Name of the regional mutual aid compact or group under which this request is coordinated (e.g., Southeast Electric Exchange, Mid-Atlantic Mutual Aid Group, Midwest Mutual Aid Group). Identifies the regional coordination framework.
reference: EEI Mutual Assistance Program; NERC EOP-005-3; regional ESOP compacts

description: Date and time when the mutual aid request was formally submitted by the requesting utility. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the request lifecycle.
Externally-known business identifier for the mutual aid request, used in inter-utility communications, ESOP documentation, and regulatory reporting. Format: MAR-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; ESOP (Emergency Support Operations Plan) standard numbering

description: Current lifecycle state of the mutual aid request, tracking progression from initial submission through crew mobilization, active deployment, and final demobilization or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|committed|active|demobilized|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this utility is the requesting party (outbound — this utility is seeking aid from a neighbor) or the responding party (inbound — this utility is providing aid to a neighbor).
reference: NERC EOP-005-3; ESOP bilateral aid agreements

description: Name of the primary operational contact at the requesting utility responsible for coordinating the mutual aid request and managing incoming crews.
reference: ESOP contact management; EEI Mutual Assistance Program

description: Direct phone number for the primary operational contact at the requesting utility. Used for real-time coordination during storm response.
reference: ESOP contact management; EEI Mutual Assistance Program

description: Legal name of the utility that is requesting mutual aid resources. Identifies the party in need of crew or equipment support during a major outage or storm event.

business_glossary_term: Requesting Utility NERC (North American Electric Reliability Corporation) ID
description: NERC-assigned registered entity identifier for the requesting utility, used for regulatory reporting and inter-utility coordination under NERC EOP standards.
Name of the primary operational contact at the responding utility responsible for coordinating crew dispatch and resource commitment.
reference: ESOP contact management; EEI Mutual Assistance Program

description: Direct phone number for the primary operational contact at the responding utility. Used for real-time coordination during crew mobilization.
reference: ESOP contact management; EEI Mutual Assistance Program

description: Legal name of the utility that is committing resources in response to the mutual aid request. Identifies the aid-providing party.

business_glossary_term: Responding Utility NERC (North American Electric Reliability Corporation) ID
description: NERC-assigned registered entity identifier for the responding (aid-providing) utility, used for regulatory reporting and inter-utility coordination.
Two-letter US state code for the primary service territory where restoration work will be performed. Used for jurisdictional compliance, state PUC reporting, and logistics planning.
reference: State PUC regulatory reporting; NERC EOP-005-3

description: Name or address of the staging area designated for incoming mutual aid crews within the requesting utility's service territory. Used for logistics coordination and crew check-in management.
reference: ESOP staging and logistics procedures; EEI Mutual Assistance Program

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the switching order record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: NERC FAC-001; GE PowerOn ADMS Switching Management Module

description: Identifier of the primary distribution feeder circuit affected by this switching order. Used for network topology analysis, outage impact assessment, and GIS integration with Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; GE PowerOn ADMS; IEEE Std 1366

Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: Switching orders require DER isolation verification before energization per IEEE 1547 and utility operating procedures. Operators must confirm DER status (online/offline/isolated) before executing swi
Reference to the substation from which the affected feeder originates or where the primary switching devices are located. Used for geographic scoping and asset management integration.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; GE PowerOn ADMS; NERC FAC-001

description: Reference to the parent outage event that triggered this switching order. Links the switching sequence to the originating interruption or planned maintenance event in the Outage Management System (OMS).
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS Module; NERC FAC-002

description: Foreign key linking to grid.grid_switching_order. Business justification: Outage switching orders may reference or be coordinated with grid switching orders for transmission-connected substations. Linking enables cross-system switching coordination and safety compliance.

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Switching orders on critical infrastructure require compliance documentation under NERC CIP or operational reliability standards. Operations must document adherence to switching protocols mandated by

Foreign key linking to grid.operating_limit. Business justification: Switching orders must be validated against operating limits (thermal/voltage) before execution. NERC FAC-001/FAC-002 compliance requires operators to verify limits are not exceeded during planned de-e

Foreign key linking to outage.planned_outage_window. Business justification: The outage_switching_order table has planned_outage_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the switching order is for a planned outage, but no FK to the planned_outage_window record. Switching orders are the prima

Foreign key linking to interconnection.poi_specification. Business justification: Switching orders affecting DER interconnection points must reference POI specifications for proper isolation procedures, disconnect switch operation, metering configuration, and safe re-energization s

business_glossary_term: Primary Switching Asset Registry Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Switching orders specify which physical switching device must be operated to isolate circuits. Safety clearance procedures require explicit asset identification. Operations teams need asset-specific o

Foreign key linking to grid.state_estimation_run. Business justification: Outage switching orders reference the state estimation snapshot used to validate network conditions at time of execution. NERC TOP-001 requires operators to use current network state before switching.

Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: The outage_switching_order table has storm_event_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the switching order is storm-related, but no FK to the storm_event master record. During storm response, switching orders are

Foreign key linking to forecast.weather_input. Business justification: Switching orders executed during adverse weather require real-time weather context for crew safety protocols, hazard assessment, and operational decision-making. Weather conditions directly impact swi

The confirmed number of customer service points that were interrupted as a result of this switching order, validated post-restoration. Used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric calculation and regulatory reporting to State PUCs.
reference: IEEE Std 1366; State PUC Reliability Reporting; NERC FAC-001

description: The actual date and time when the final switching step was completed and the switching order was closed. Used for outage duration calculation and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability performance reporting.
reference: IEEE Std 1366; NERC FAC-001; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: The actual date and time when the field crew began executing the first step of the switching sequence. Used for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reliability metric calculation and post-event analysis.
reference: IEEE Std 1366; NERC FAC-001; State PUC Reliability Reporting

business_glossary_term: Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS) Switching Order Reference
description: The native switching order identifier assigned by the GE PowerOn Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Silver Layer lakehouse and the operational ADMS source system.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS Switching Management Module

description: Free-text notes documenting the approval chain for this switching order, including any conditional approvals, escalations, or deviations from standard authorization procedures. Supports NERC compliance audit trail.

description: The date and time when this switching order record was most recently modified. Tracks any changes to the order after initial creation, supporting audit trail requirements.

description: Human-readable description of the distribution or transmission network segment affected by this switching order, including feeder name, circuit section, and geographic reference. Supports field crew orientation and post-event reporting.

Voltage classification of the network segment predicted to be affected. Determines the severity tier, crew qualification requirements, and regulatory reporting category.
reference: IEC 60038 — Standard Voltages; IEEE C57; NERC FAC Standards

description: Standardized code representing the weather conditions at the time of prediction creation (e.g., clear, wind, ice, lightning, flood). Used for cause analysis, storm classification, and reliability reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|wind|ice|lightning|flood|snow|extreme_heat|fog|tornado|hurricane — promote to reference product]
reference: IEEE 1366 — Major Event Day Classification; NOAA Weather Classification

description: Recorded or forecast wind speed in miles per hour at the predicted fault location at the time of prediction creation. Used for storm severity assessment and major event day (MED) classification.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Major Event Day Classification; NOAA

description: Primary key for reliability_index_period

Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: SAIDI/SAIFI reliability indices are computed and reported per control area for NERC BAL and PUC regulatory filings. The control area is the primary reporting boundary for reliability performance. Doma

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reporting is mandated by specific PUC obligations with defined performance thresholds and filing deadlines. Regulatory affairs maps each reporting period to the governing obligation

Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Reliability indices (SAIDI, SAIFI, CAIDI) are calculated and reported by forecast zone for state PUC regulatory compliance and performance benchmarking. Forecast zones define geographic reporting boun

Free-text description of the reason for amending or recomputing this reliability index period record (e.g., data correction from OMS, reclassification of MED events, customer count correction). Required for version_number greater than 1. Supports regulatory audit trail and PUC amendment filing documentation.
reference: State PUC Amendment Reporting Requirements; SOX Internal Controls

description: Identifier or name of the authorized utility employee (typically a Reliability Engineer or Director of Grid Operations) who approved this reliability index period record for regulatory filing. Supports SOX internal control documentation and regulatory audit requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302 and 906 (Management Certification); State PUC Audit Requirements; NERC Compliance

description: Date and time when this reliability index period record was formally approved for regulatory filing. Supports SOX internal control audit trails and regulatory submission documentation.
reference: SOX Section 302; State PUC Audit Requirements; NERC Compliance Documentation

business_glossary_term: Customer Average Interruption Duration Index (CAIDI) in Minutes
description: Customer Average Interruption Duration Index expressed in minutes per interruption. Computed as total_customer_minutes_interrupted divided by total_customer_interruptions (equivalently, SAIDI divided by SAIFI). Represents the average duration of each interruption experienced by affected customers. Used to assess restoration performance.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.3; NERC Reliability Standards; State PUC Reporting Requirements

business_glossary_term: IEEE 1366 Normalized CAIDI (Excluding Major Event Days) in Minutes
description: CAIDI value computed after excluding Major Event Day (MED) interruptions per IEEE 1366 normalization methodology. Derived from normalized SAIDI and SAIFI values. Used for PUC performance standard compliance and restoration performance benchmarking.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.4; State PUC Performance Standards

description: Identifies the specific computational methodology applied to derive the reliability indices in this record. IEEE_1366_STANDARD applies the full IEEE 1366 methodology including MEDs; IEEE_1366_MED_EXCLUDED applies the normalized MED-excluded methodology; PUC_CUSTOM applies a state-specific PUC-mandated variation; NERC_BENCHMARK applies NERC benchmarking methodology.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; State PUC Reporting Requirements; NERC Reliability Standards

description: Date and time when this reliability index period record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX internal control requirements.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; IEEE Std 1366-2012; State PUC Audit Requirements

description: Total number of customers served within the reporting entity boundary during the reporting period. This is the denominator used in SAIDI and SAIFI index calculations per IEEE 1366. Typically represents the average or end-of-period customer count as defined by the applicable PUC methodology.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.1; State PUC Reporting Requirements

description: Number of outage events during the reporting period caused by equipment failure (excluding storm and third-party causes). Supports asset reliability analysis, maintenance planning, and capital investment justification for aging infrastructure programs.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; Ventyx Asset Suite Work Management; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Date and time when this reliability index period record was most recently modified. Tracks amendments, recomputations, and corrections to the reliability indices. Supports audit trail and regulatory amendment documentation.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; State PUC Amendment Reporting Requirements; IEEE Std 1366-2012

description: Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index computed as total momentary customer interruptions divided by customers served. Measures the frequency of brief power interruptions (five minutes or less) per customer. Increasingly required by PUCs as a power quality metric alongside SAIDI and SAIFI.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.5; State PUC Power Quality Reporting Requirements

description: Number of calendar days during the reporting period that qualified as Major Event Days (MEDs) per the IEEE 1366 beta + 2.5 sigma threshold methodology. Used to assess storm and extreme weather impact on reliability performance.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.4; State PUC Reporting Requirements

business_glossary_term: Major Event Day (MED) Excluded Customer Interruptions
description: Total customer interruptions attributable to Major Event Days (MEDs) that are excluded from the normalized IEEE 1366 index calculations. MEDs are days where the daily SAIDI exceeds a statistically derived threshold (beta + 2.5 sigma). Tracking this separately enables both total and MED-excluded reporting as required by most PUCs.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.4 (Major Event Day definition); State PUC Reporting Requirements

business_glossary_term: Major Event Day (MED) Excluded Customer Minutes Interrupted
description: Total customer minutes interrupted attributable to Major Event Days (MEDs) that are excluded from the normalized IEEE 1366 index calculations. Used to compute MED-normalized SAIDI and SAIFI values required for PUC regulatory filings and NERC benchmarking.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.4; State PUC Reporting Requirements; NERC Reliability Standards

business_glossary_term: Major Event Day (MED) SAIDI Threshold in Minutes
description: The computed daily SAIDI threshold (beta + 2.5 sigma) used to classify a calendar day as a Major Event Day for this reporting period. Derived from the five-year historical daily SAIDI distribution per IEEE 1366 methodology. Documents the MED classification boundary applied to this period.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.4; State PUC Reporting Requirements

description: Total number of momentary interruptions (duration five minutes or less per IEEE 1366 definition) experienced by customers during the reporting period. Used to compute MAIFI (Momentary Average Interruption Frequency Index) and assess power quality performance.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.5 (MAIFI definition); State PUC Power Quality Reporting Requirements

business_glossary_term: North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC) Registered Entity ID
description: NERC Compliance Registry identifier for the utility entity responsible for this reliability report. Required for NERC reliability standard compliance submissions and benchmarking participation. Aligns with the NERC Transmission Planner, Distribution Provider, or Load-Serving Entity registration.
reference: NERC Compliance Registry; NERC Reliability Standards EOP-004; FERC Order 693

business_glossary_term: Outage Management System (OMS) Source System Code
description: Identifier of the Outage Management System (OMS) instance from which the underlying outage event data was sourced for this reliability index computation. Supports data lineage tracing back to GE PowerOn ADMS or equivalent OMS. Critical for audit and regulatory filing validation.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS Integration; SOX Data Lineage Requirements; State PUC Audit Requirements

description: Calendar date marking the inclusive end of the reliability reporting period. Combined with period_start_date to define the exact window over which SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI indices are computed.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; FERC Form 1; State PUC Annual Reliability Report

description: Calendar date marking the inclusive start of the reliability reporting period. Combined with period_end_date to define the exact window over which SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI indices are computed.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; FERC Form 1; State PUC Annual Reliability Report

description: Classification of the reporting period granularity. Determines the aggregation window for SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI index calculations. MONTHLY is used for operational monitoring; QUARTERLY and ANNUAL are used for PUC regulatory filings; ROLLING_12_MONTH is used for NERC benchmarking.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4; NERC Reliability Standards EOP-004; State PUC Reporting Requirements

description: Customer minutes interrupted attributable to planned (scheduled maintenance) outage events during the reporting period. Some PUC jurisdictions require separate reporting of planned vs. unplanned interruptions. Enables decomposition of total CMI into planned and unplanned components.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 3.2 (planned interruption definition); State PUC Reporting Requirements

description: SAIDI value from the immediately preceding equivalent reporting period (e.g., prior month, prior quarter, prior year) for the same reporting entity. Stored on the record to support period-over-period variance analysis in regulatory filings without requiring a self-join.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; State PUC Annual Reliability Report; NERC Reliability Benchmarking

description: SAIFI value from the immediately preceding equivalent reporting period for the same reporting entity. Stored on the record to support period-over-period variance analysis in regulatory filings without requiring a self-join.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; State PUC Annual Reliability Report; NERC Reliability Benchmarking

description: Two-letter state code plus PUC suffix identifying the state Public Utility Commission jurisdiction under which this reliability index period record is filed (e.g., CA-PUC, NY-PUC, TX-PUC). Determines applicable performance standards, reporting formats, and filing deadlines.
reference: State Public Utility Commission Regulations; NERC Compliance Registry; FERC Jurisdictional Boundaries

business_glossary_term: Public Utility Commission (PUC) SAIDI Performance Target in Minutes
description: Regulatory SAIDI performance target (in minutes) established by the applicable state PUC for the reporting period and reporting entity. Used to assess compliance with performance-based rate mechanisms and potential penalty or incentive triggers. Null if no PUC target applies to this reporting level.
reference: State PUC Performance-Based Ratemaking Orders; State PUC Reliability Standards

business_glossary_term: Public Utility Commission (PUC) SAIFI Performance Target
description: Regulatory SAIFI performance target established by the applicable state PUC for the reporting period and reporting entity. Used to assess compliance with performance-based rate mechanisms and potential penalty or incentive triggers. Null if no PUC target applies to this reporting level.
reference: State PUC Performance-Based Ratemaking Orders; State PUC Reliability Standards

business_glossary_term: Public Utility Commission (PUC) Performance Target Met Indicator
description: Indicates whether the utility met all applicable PUC reliability performance targets (SAIDI and SAIFI) for this reporting period and reporting entity. True if all applicable targets were met; False if one or more targets were missed. Null if no PUC targets apply. Drives performance-based rate adjustment calculations.
reference: State PUC Performance-Based Ratemaking Orders; State PUC Reliability Standards

description: Business identifier for the specific entity (circuit ID, substation code, PUC territory code, operating area code, or system-level identifier) at which the reliability indices are reported. Corresponds to the reporting_level classification. For SYSTEM level, this is the utility NERC registered entity identifier.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; NERC Compliance Registry; State PUC Service Territory Definitions

description: Human-readable name of the reporting entity (e.g., circuit name, substation name, PUC territory name, operating area name). Used for display in regulatory filings and reliability performance dashboards.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; State PUC Reporting Requirements

description: Organizational or geographic granularity at which the reliability indices are computed and reported. SYSTEM represents the full utility service territory; CIRCUIT and SUBSTATION represent distribution-level granularity; PUC_TERRITORY aligns with regulatory jurisdiction boundaries; OPERATING_AREA represents internal operational divisions.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 5; State PUC Reporting Requirements; NERC Reliability Standards

description: Externally-known business identifier for the reporting period, encoding the period type and calendar reference (e.g., MONTHLY-2024-03, QUARTERLY-2024-Q1, ANNUAL-2024). Used as the primary business key for regulatory filings and cross-system reconciliation.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; NERC Reliability Standards; State PUC Reporting Requirements

business_glossary_term: System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI) in Minutes
description: System Average Interruption Duration Index expressed in minutes per customer. Computed as total_customer_minutes_interrupted divided by customers_served. Represents the average total duration of interruptions experienced per customer during the reporting period. Primary reliability metric for PUC regulatory filings and NERC benchmarking.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.1; NERC Reliability Standards; State PUC Reporting Requirements

business_glossary_term: IEEE 1366 Normalized SAIDI (Excluding Major Event Days) in Minutes
description: SAIDI value computed after excluding Major Event Day (MED) interruptions per IEEE 1366 normalization methodology. Computed as (total_customer_minutes_interrupted minus med_excluded_customer_minutes) divided by customers_served. This is the primary metric used for year-over-year performance benchmarking and PUC performance standard compliance.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.4; State PUC Performance Standards; NERC Reliability Benchmarking

description: System Average Interruption Frequency Index expressed as interruptions per customer. Computed as total_customer_interruptions divided by customers_served. Represents the average number of interruptions experienced per customer during the reporting period. Key reliability metric for PUC regulatory filings.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.2; NERC Reliability Standards; State PUC Reporting Requirements

business_glossary_term: IEEE 1366 Normalized SAIFI (Excluding Major Event Days)
description: SAIFI value computed after excluding Major Event Day (MED) interruptions per IEEE 1366 normalization methodology. Computed as (total_customer_interruptions minus med_excluded_customer_interruptions) divided by customers_served. Used for PUC performance standard compliance and NERC benchmarking.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.4; State PUC Performance Standards; NERC Reliability Benchmarking

description: Number of outage events during the reporting period that were classified as storm-related (weather-caused). Supports storm impact analysis, MED classification validation, and regulatory storm reporting requirements. Sourced from OMS event cause codes.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.4; State PUC Storm Reporting Requirements; NERC EOP-004

description: Number of outage events during the reporting period caused by third parties (e.g., vehicle accidents, dig-ins, animal contacts, vegetation encroachment by non-utility parties). Some PUC jurisdictions allow exclusion of third-party caused outages from performance standard calculations.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; State PUC Reporting Requirements; NERC EOP-004

description: Total number of customer interruptions (sum of customers affected across all outage events) during the reporting period. This is the numerator for SAIFI calculation. Each customer affected by each distinct outage event counts as one interruption.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.2 (SAIFI definition); State PUC Reporting Requirements

description: Sum of the product of customers affected multiplied by outage duration in minutes across all outage events in the reporting period. This is the numerator for SAIDI calculation, expressed in customer-minutes. Also referred to as Customer Minutes of Interruption (CMI) in regulatory filings.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 4.1 (SAIDI definition); State PUC Reporting Requirements; NERC Reliability Standards

description: Total number of distinct outage events (interruption incidents) that occurred within the reporting entity boundary during the reporting period. Provides context for interpreting SAIDI and SAIFI values and supports frequency-based reliability analysis.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012; GE PowerOn ADMS OMS Event Records; State PUC Reporting Requirements

description: Customer minutes interrupted attributable to unplanned (forced/fault) outage events during the reporting period. Unplanned interruptions are the primary driver of reliability index degradation and are the focus of most PUC performance standards.
reference: IEEE Std 1366-2012 Section 3.2; State PUC Performance Standards; NERC Reliability Standards

description: Sequential version number incremented each time this reliability index period record is amended or recomputed. Version 1 represents the initial filing; subsequent versions represent amendments. Supports regulatory amendment tracking and audit trail requirements.
reference: State PUC Amendment Reporting Requirements; SOX Internal Controls; IEEE Std 1366-2012

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each discrete restoration action record within the outage management system. Primary key for the restoration_action data product.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — Outage Management System (OMS) Action Record

description: Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Restoration actions change load/generation balance within control areas, requiring AGC adjustments and interchange schedule updates. Essential for coordinating distribution restoration with transmissi

Foreign key linking to interconnection.der_system. Business justification: FLISR and manual restoration sequences must account for DER presence on segments (anti-islanding protection, voltage regulation impact, fault current contribution). Restoration logic checks DER status
Identifier of the substation serving the network segment affected by this restoration action. Used for substation-level outage aggregation and reliability reporting.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS

description: Foreign key linking to grid.ems_node. Business justification: Restoration actions target specific EMS network nodes/buses. The EMS node identifies the precise network element being restored in the power flow model. Domain experts expect this link to correlate fi
Reference to the parent outage event that this restoration action is associated with. Links the discrete action to the broader outage lifecycle managed in the OMS.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — Outage Management System (OMS)

description: Identifier of the distribution feeder circuit on which this restoration action was performed, as defined in the GIS and ADMS network model. Used for feeder-level reliability performance tracking.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network — Network Modeling; GE PowerOn ADMS

description: Foreign key linking to asset.registry. Business justification: Restoration actions document which switching assets (breakers, reclosers, switches) were operated during outage restoration. Field crews log asset operations for post-event analysis, maintenance trigg
Reference to the switching order that authorized or directed this restoration action, if applicable. Switching orders govern the sequence of switching operations for safe restoration.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — Switching Order Management

description: Foreign key linking to interconnection.poi_specification. Business justification: Restoration actions at DER points of interconnection must reference POI specifications for voltage level, phase configuration, protection relay settings, and proper switching sequence to prevent equip

Reference to the distribution network segment (feeder section, lateral, or zone) affected by this restoration action. Identifies the portion of the grid being isolated, sectionalized, or re-energized.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network — Network Modeling; GE PowerOn ADMS — Distribution Management System (DMS)

description: Foreign key linking to grid.scada_point. Business justification: Restoration actions involve SCADA-monitored devices (reclosers, switches). Linking enables real-time status verification, FLISR automation, and coordinated distribution automation with EMS operations.

Identifier of the switching device (recloser, sectionalizer, circuit breaker, or fuse) operated to isolate the faulted segment during this action. References the device asset in the GIS or ADMS network model.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network — Asset Location; GE PowerOn ADMS — ADMS Network Model

description: Foreign key linking to outage.storm_event. Business justification: The restoration_action table captures discrete restoration steps during outage events. During storm events, restoration actions are part of coordinated storm response and must be traceable to the stor

Foreign key linking to forecast.weather_input. Business justification: Restoration crews require real-time and forecasted weather data for safety planning, equipment selection, and estimated time of restoration (ETR) accuracy. Weather conditions directly affect restorati
Timestamp when the restoration action was confirmed complete and verified by the crew or automated system. Used to calculate action duration and assess restoration efficiency.
Elapsed time in minutes from action initiation (action_timestamp) to action completion (action_completed_timestamp). Used in CAIDI calculations and crew performance benchmarking.
reference: IEEE 1366 — CAIDI; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Free-text field for operator or crew notes describing the specific conditions, complications, or observations encountered during this restoration action. Supports post-event analysis and lessons-learned documentation.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS; NERC FAC-002 Post-Event Analysis

description: Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this restoration action as recorded in the OMS or switching order log (e.g., RA-2024-00451). Used by field crews, control room operators, and regulators to reference specific actions.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Action Reference; NERC FAC-002

description: Ordinal sequence number of this action within the parent outage event's restoration workflow. Enables step-by-step reconstruction of the restoration sequence for post-event analysis and SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI reporting.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Power Distribution Reliability Indices; GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS

description: Current lifecycle status of the restoration action. Pending: scheduled but not yet started. In_progress: crew or automation is actively executing. Completed: action successfully finished. Failed: action attempted but unsuccessful. Cancelled: action voided before execution.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS Workflow; NERC CIP-014

description: The principal real-world timestamp when this restoration action was executed or initiated in the field or by the automated system. Used as the primary event time for SAIDI/SAIFI/CAIDI calculations and restoration timeline analysis.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Power Distribution Reliability Indices; NERC FAC-002

description: Classification of the type of restoration action performed. Isolation: de-energizes a faulted segment. Sectionalizing: divides a feeder to limit the outage zone. Load transfer: moves load to an alternate feeder. Temporary bypass: restores power via a temporary path. Permanent repair: physical repair restoring normal configuration. FLISR automated: Fault Location Isolation and Service Restoration (FLISR) algorithm-driven action.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — FLISR; IEEE 1366 — Power Distribution Reliability Indices

description: Timestamp when this restoration action record was first created in the OMS or data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

Timestamp when the field crew arrived on-site to begin executing this manual restoration action. Used to calculate crew travel time and on-site restoration duration for workforce performance analytics.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — Crew Dispatch; Ventyx Asset Suite — Work Management

description: Timestamp when the field crew was dispatched from the control room or dispatch center to execute this restoration action. Used to measure dispatch-to-arrival interval and overall crew response time.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — Crew Dispatch; Ventyx Asset Suite — Work Management

description: Indicates whether a customer notification (outage alert, ETR update, or restoration confirmation) was sent to affected customers as a result of this restoration action. Supports compliance with state PUC customer communication requirements.
reference: State PUC Customer Notification Requirements; Oracle Utilities CC&B — Customer Management

description: Total number of customer service points that were de-energized or affected by the segment targeted by this action at the time of action execution. Provides context for partial vs. full restoration assessment.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Power Distribution Reliability Indices; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Number of customers within the affected segment that could not be restored by FLISR automation and required manual crew dispatch for restoration. Highlights FLISR coverage gaps.

Number of customers restored through fully automated Fault Location Isolation and Service Restoration (FLISR) self-healing actions without manual crew intervention. Key metric for FLISR performance analytics.
Number of customer service points restored to power as a direct result of this specific restoration action. Used in SAIDI, SAIFI, and CAIDI index calculations and regulatory reliability reporting.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Power Distribution Reliability Indices; State PUC Reliability Reporting Requirements

description: Estimated Time to Restore (ETR) for the remaining affected customers at the time this action was recorded. Updated as each restoration action is completed to refine the ETR communicated to customers and regulators.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS ETR; State PUC Customer Notification Requirements

description: Indicates whether the restoration action was executed manually by a field crew or control room operator, fully automated by the FLISR algorithm, or semi-automated (system-initiated but operator-confirmed).
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — Advanced Distribution Management System (ADMS); IEEE 1366

description: Timestamp when the fault triggering this restoration action was first detected by SCADA, FLISR, or field crew. Distinct from action_timestamp; used to calculate fault-to-isolation and fault-to-restoration intervals.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — FLISR; OSIsoft PI — SCADA Integration; NERC FAC-002

description: Version identifier of the Fault Location Isolation and Service Restoration (FLISR) algorithm that executed this automated restoration action. Enables performance benchmarking across algorithm versions and supports software change management.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — FLISR Module; IEEE 1366

description: Number of automated switching operations performed by the FLISR algorithm to isolate the faulted segment during this restoration action. Supports FLISR performance analytics and switching operation audits.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — FLISR; NERC FAC-002

business_glossary_term: FLISR Load Transfer Actions Taken Count
description: Number of automated load transfer switching operations performed by the FLISR algorithm to restore power to non-faulted segments during this action. Key metric for FLISR self-healing effectiveness.

Outcome of the FLISR automated restoration attempt for this action. Success: FLISR fully restored the affected segment. Partial_success: FLISR restored a portion; manual intervention required for remainder. Failure: FLISR could not restore; full manual restoration required. Not_applicable: action was manual, not FLISR-driven.

Indicates whether this restoration action occurred during a Major Event Day (MED) as defined by IEEE 1366 beta method. MED events are typically excluded from standard SAIDI/SAIFI calculations and reported separately to regulators.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Major Event Day (MED) Beta Method; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Indicates whether this action resulted in a partial restoration (True) — restoring power to a subset of affected customers — or a full restoration of the affected segment (False). Drives ETR refinement and customer notification logic.
reference: IEEE 1366 — Power Distribution Reliability Indices; GE PowerOn ADMS — OMS

description: Indicates whether this restoration action is associated with a planned maintenance outage (True) or an unplanned/forced outage (False). Planned outages are tracked separately for SAIDI/SAIFI reporting and customer notification compliance.
reference: IEEE 1366; State PUC Planned Outage Notification Requirements

description: Identifier of the source feeder or substation bus from which load is transferred during a load transfer restoration action. Populated when action_type is load_transfer or flisr_automated.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — ADMS Load Transfer; IEEE C37.100

description: Identifier of the target feeder or substation bus receiving the transferred load during a load transfer restoration action. Used to assess feeder loading and capacity constraints post-transfer.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — ADMS Load Transfer; IEEE C37.100

description: Source system reference key for this restoration action as recorded in the GE PowerOn ADMS Outage Management System (OMS). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational OMS.

Standardized cause code for the fault or outage condition that necessitated this restoration action (e.g., equipment failure, tree contact, animal contact, storm, vehicle accident, planned maintenance). Aligns with IEEE 1366 cause code taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|tree_contact|animal_contact|storm|vehicle_accident|planned_maintenance|unknown|other — promote to reference product]
reference: IEEE 1366 — Power Distribution Reliability Indices; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Indicates whether this restoration action is associated with an outage event that must be reported to a regulatory body (NERC, FERC, State PUC, or DOE). Drives inclusion in regulatory reliability reports and OE-417 emergency incident filings.
reference: NERC EOP-004 — Event Reporting; DOE OE-417 Emergency Incident Reporting; State PUC Reliability Reporting

description: Reference identifier linking this restoration action to the corresponding SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) event or alarm in OSIsoft PI historian. Enables correlation between OMS restoration records and real-time grid telemetry.
reference: OSIsoft PI — SCADA Integration; NERC CIP-007

description: FK to outage.crew_dispatch.crew_dispatch_id — Each restoration action is performed by a dispatched crew. Links action to the crew that executed it for accountability and performance tracking.

FK to outage.crew_dispatch.crew_dispatch_id — Restoration actions are performed by dispatched crews. This FK links the action to the crew that executed it, enabling crew performance tracking and accountability.
FK to outage.outage_event.outage_event_id — Every restoration action is performed within the context of an outage event. Required for step-by-step restoration tracking and ETR refinement.

Magnitude of electrical load in Megawatts (MW) transferred from the faulted segment to an alternate feeder during a load transfer action. Used for grid capacity analysis and post-event load flow validation.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS — ADMS; IEEE C37.100; NERC TPL standards

description: Timestamp when this restoration action record was last modified. Tracks updates to status, customer counts, or FLISR outcome fields throughout the restoration lifecycle.

Nominal voltage level in kilovolts (kV) of the network segment on which this restoration action was performed (e.g., 4.16, 12.47, 34.5, 69, 115 kV). Used to classify the action within transmission vs. distribution tiers.
Unique surrogate identifier for the storm event record in the Outage Management System (OMS). Primary key for the storm_event entity.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS Entity Model

description: Foreign key linking to grid.control_area. Business justification: Storm events are scoped to a control area for NERC OE-417 reporting and balancing authority coordination. The control area defines the operational boundary for storm response and mutual aid activation

Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Storm events impact specific forecast zones. Utilities track zone-level storm impacts for Major Event Day (MED) threshold calculations, mutual aid resource allocation, and regulatory reporting (IEEE 1

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Major storm events trigger specific regulatory reporting obligations (NERC EOP-004 emergency operations, state PUC emergency reporting requirements). Compliance teams track which obligations are activ

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Major storm events trigger mandatory DOE OE-417 emergency filings and PUC major event day reports. Storm_event tracks oe417_filing_required and oe417_filing_timestamp but lacks FK to the actual filing
Reference to the primary service territory affected by the storm event. Links to the geographic service area definition used for regulatory reporting and customer impact scoping.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; PUC Service Territory Filings

description: Foreign key linking to forecast.weather_input. Business justification: Storm event severity tracking and post-event analysis require actual weather data (temperature, wind speed, precipitation) for regulatory reporting, insurance claims, and operational review. Weather i
Number of distribution circuits (below 69kV) that experienced outages or damage during this storm event. Key metric for distribution storm damage assessment and restoration prioritization.
reference: GE PowerOn ADMS OMS; PUC Storm Reporting; Esri ArcGIS Utility Network

description: Free-text description of the geographic area impacted by the storm, including counties, cities, or distribution circuits affected. Supplements the service territory reference for regulatory filings and public communications.
reference: PUC Storm Reporting Requirements; NERC Event Analysis

description: Number of transmission lines (69kV and above) that experienced outages or damage during this storm event. Used for NERC reliability reporting and transmission damage assessment.
reference: NERC Event Analysis Guidelines; FERC Form 1; Esri ArcGIS Utility Network

description: Document management reference number or URL for the post-storm After-Action Report (AAR) filed following storm demobilization. Used for continuous improvement tracking and regulatory compliance.
reference: PUC Storm Reporting Requirements; Utility Emergency Response Plan; NERC Event Analysis

description: The date and time this storm event record was first created in the OMS or data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Indicates whether this curtailment event qualifies for financial compensation or credit under the applicable PPA or regulatory framework.
Total duration of the curtailment event in minutes, calculated from start to end timestamp.

Detailed narrative explanation of the specific reason for the curtailment event, including operational context and decision rationale.

Current lifecycle status of the curtailment event: initiated (instruction issued), active (curtailment in effect), restored (generation resumed), cancelled (instruction withdrawn), or expired (time limit reached).

Classification of the curtailment reason: economic (negative pricing, market conditions), reliability (grid stability), transmission constraint (congestion), regulatory (compliance mandate), environmental (permit limits), or operational (equipment limitation).
The operational system of record that originated this curtailment event data (e.g., DERMS, ADMS, SCADA, EMS).
The estimated financial impact in US dollars of the curtailed energy, calculated using applicable market prices or PPA rates at the time of curtailment.
The percentage variance between forecasted and actual available generation capacity at the time of curtailment, used for forecast model improvement.

The specific grid constraint or limitation that triggered the curtailment: transmission congestion, voltage limit, frequency deviation, thermal overload, stability limit, or none if not constraint-driven.

description: Origin of the curtailment instruction: ISO/RTO dispatch, ADMS (Advanced Distribution Management System), DERMS (Distributed Energy Resource Management System), manual operator, market signal, or automatic control system.

Indicates whether a post-event operational review has been completed to analyze the curtailment decision and outcomes.

Ratio of real power to apparent power during the interval. Must meet interconnection agreement requirements typically between 0.95 leading and 0.95 lagging.
Indicates whether this read is included in PPA settlement calculations. False for reads with poor data quality or outside contract delivery periods.
Reactive power measured during the interval in kilovolt-amperes reactive. Required for power factor compliance and grid stability analysis.

Source system that captured or provided the meter read data. Indicates data provenance for audit and quality validation.

Battery energy storage system state of charge at interval end as percentage of total capacity. Applicable to battery storage assets.
Time zone of the meter read timestamp. Critical for accurate interval alignment across facilities in different time zones.
Timestamp when the VEE validation process was completed for this read. Used for data quality audit trails.
Data quality status from the VEE process indicating whether the read is actual, estimated, or edited. Critical for REC issuance eligibility and settlement accuracy.
Average voltage at the point of interconnection during the interval in kilovolts. Used for power quality monitoring and interconnection compliance.
Average wind speed at hub height during the interval. Applicable to wind generation facilities for capacity factor analysis.
