description: The date and time when the billing determinants were calculated from validated meter interval data. Represents the business event time when MDM output was transformed into billing inputs.

Free-text field for billing analysts to document special circumstances, data quality issues, manual adjustments, or other notes relevant to this billing determinant record. Used for audit trail and knowledge transfer.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; SAP IS-U Billing

description: The contracted maximum demand level agreed upon between the utility and customer. Used for rate schedules where customers commit to a minimum demand level in exchange for favorable pricing. Excess demand above this level may incur penalty charges.
reference: FERC Rate Design; PUC Commercial Rate Schedules

description: Date and time when this billing determinant record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; SAP IS-U Audit

description: Energy consumption during Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) event hours. Used for CPP rate schedules where utilities invoke premium pricing during grid stress events (typically 10-15 days per year).
reference: FERC CPP Rate Design; PUC Demand Response Programs

description: Composite score (0-100) representing the quality of the underlying meter interval data used to calculate these determinants. Considers factors such as percentage of actual vs estimated readings, validation rule pass rate, and data completeness. Used to flag determinants requiring manual review.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM VEE; SAP IS-U Device Management

description: Number of meter intervals that were estimated rather than actual readings. Used for data quality assessment and to flag billing determinants that may require review due to missing or invalid meter data.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM VEE; ANSI C12.19

description: Number of meter interval readings used to calculate these billing determinants. Typically 2,880 intervals for 15-minute data over a 30-day period, or 1,440 intervals for 30-minute data. Used for data quality validation.

Flag indicating whether this is the final, validated billing determinant record for the billing period (true) or a preliminary/estimated version (false). Used to prevent duplicate billing and ensure only validated determinants are passed to the billing engine.
reference: Oracle CC&B Billing; SAP IS-U Billing

description: Date and time when this billing determinant record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and identifying records that have been manually adjusted after initial calculation.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; SAP IS-U Audit

description: Ratio of average load to peak load during the billing period, calculated as (total_kwh / (peak_demand_kw * billing_hours)). Indicates how efficiently the customer uses their peak capacity. Values range from 0 to 1, with higher values indicating more consistent usage patterns.
Energy consumption during mid-peak TOU periods (shoulder hours between on-peak and off-peak). Used for TOU rate schedules with three-tier time-based pricing.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this billing determinant record. Used for audit trail and accountability in SOX compliance reporting.

Energy exported to the grid from customer-owned Distributed Energy Resources (DER) such as rooftop solar. Used for Net Energy Metering (NEM) rate schedules where exported energy is credited against consumption or compensated at a specified rate.
reference: PURPA; State NEM Regulations; FERC Order 2222

description: Energy imported from the grid by customers with Distributed Energy Resources (DER). Used for Net Energy Metering (NEM) rate schedules to calculate net consumption after accounting for on-site generation.
reference: PURPA; State NEM Regulations; FERC Order 2222

description: Maximum power demand during off-peak TOU periods. Used for rate schedules that apply separate demand charges for off-peak periods.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: Energy consumption during off-peak TOU periods (typically nights and weekends when grid demand is lower). Used for TOU rate calculations with discounted pricing.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: Maximum power demand during on-peak TOU periods. Used for rate schedules that apply separate demand charges for on-peak versus off-peak periods.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: Energy consumption during on-peak TOU periods (typically weekday afternoons when grid demand is highest). Used for TOU and CPP rate calculations with premium pricing.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: Maximum instantaneous power demand in kilowatts recorded during the billing period. Used for demand-based rate schedules common in commercial and industrial tariffs. Typically measured as the highest 15-minute or 30-minute interval average.
Date and time when the peak demand (kW) occurred during the billing period. Used for demand response analysis, load profiling, and customer engagement regarding demand management opportunities.

Ratio of real power (kW) to apparent power (kVA) during the billing period, typically calculated at the time of peak demand. Used to assess power quality and apply power factor adjustment charges. Values range from 0 to 1, with 1.0 representing ideal unity power factor.

Demand value used for ratchet clause billing, typically a percentage (e.g., 80%) of the highest demand recorded in the previous 11 months. Used in commercial and industrial rate schedules to ensure minimum demand charges even during low-usage months.
reference: FERC Rate Design; PUC Commercial Rate Schedules

description: Maximum reactive power demand in kilovolt-amperes reactive during the billing period. Used for power factor penalties or reactive power charges in industrial and large commercial rate schedules.

System of record from which the meter interval data originated. Indicates whether data came from Meter Data Management (MDM), Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI), manual meter reading, estimation algorithms, or SCADA systems.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM; SAP IS-U Device Management

description: Energy consumption adjusted for weather variations using heating degree days (HDD) and cooling degree days (CDD). Used for load forecasting, energy efficiency program evaluation, and customer usage comparisons across different weather conditions.
Total energy consumption in kilowatt-hours for the billing period. Sum of all interval data across all Time-of-Use (TOU) periods. Primary determinant for energy charges.
Energy consumption for TOU period 1 as defined in the rate schedule. Supports rate schedules with custom TOU period definitions beyond standard on-peak/off-peak/mid-peak classifications.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: Energy consumption for TOU period 2 as defined in the rate schedule. Supports rate schedules with custom TOU period definitions beyond standard on-peak/off-peak/mid-peak classifications.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: Energy consumption for TOU period 3 as defined in the rate schedule. Supports rate schedules with custom TOU period definitions beyond standard on-peak/off-peak/mid-peak classifications.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: Energy consumption for TOU period 4 as defined in the rate schedule. Supports rate schedules with custom TOU period definitions beyond standard on-peak/off-peak/mid-peak classifications.
reference: FERC TOU Rate Design; PUC Rate Schedule

description: Current validation state of the billing determinants. Indicates whether the calculated values have passed automated validation rules, require manual review, or have been adjusted by billing analysts.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM VEE; SAP IS-U Billing

Annual interest rate applied to the deposit balance, expressed as a percentage. Rate is typically set by state PUC regulations and may be tied to treasury rates or fixed by tariff.

Actual date the deposit (plus accrued interest) was refunded to the customer or applied to their account balance. Null for deposits not yet refunded.

Date on which the deposit becomes eligible for refund to the customer, calculated based on PUC-mandated holding periods and customer payment history criteria. Typically 12-24 months of on-time payment history.

Method used to return the deposit to the customer. Account credit applies deposit to outstanding balance, check and direct deposit return funds directly to customer, wire transfer used for large amounts.

Classification of revenue type for financial and regulatory reporting. Aligns with FERC revenue categories and state regulatory reporting requirements.

Short code identifying the rider or surcharge if this component is a supplemental charge (e.g., FAC for Fuel Adjustment Clause, EEC for Energy Efficiency Charge, REC for Renewable Energy Charge). Null for base rate components.
Rounding method applied to calculated charges for this component. Round_half_up is standard commercial rounding, round_down always rounds down, round_up always rounds up, truncate drops decimals.

Start time for the TOU period in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). For example, on-peak might start at 14:00. Null for non-TOU components.

Foreign key linking to trading.contract. Business justification: Large C&I customer accounts are served under bilateral supply contracts managed in the trading/ETRM system. Contract management, rate reconciliation, and regulatory reporting (FERC EQR) require linkin

FK to customer.profile.profile_id — Every account must reference the customer profile (person or organization) that owns it. This is the most fundamental FK in any CIS — account.profile_id → profile.profile_id. Without this, you cannot

Grouping classification that determines billing consolidation and hierarchy rules. Consolidated billing groups multiple premises under one bill; corporate parent denotes the apex of a corporate hierarchy; municipal aggregation and Community Choice Aggregation (CCA) denote aggregated load programs; franchise denotes accounts under a specific franchise territory agreement.
reference: SAP IS-U Account Group (VKONT-VKGRP); Oracle CC&B Account Management Group; FERC OATT; State PUC Tariff

description: Externally-known utility account number as assigned by the Customer Information System (CIS). This is the number printed on customer bills and used in all customer-facing communications. Sourced from SAP IS-U FKKVK or Oracle CC&B ACCOUNT table.
reference: SAP IS-U Contract Account (FKKVK); Oracle CC&B Account; NAESB WEQ-001

description: Current lifecycle state of the utility account. Governs service eligibility, billing generation, and collections processing. Transitions include: pending_activation (new account awaiting energization), active (energized and billed), suspended (temporarily halted per tariff or non-payment), disconnected (service terminated), written_off (uncollectable balance), reactivated (restored after disconnection).
reference: SAP IS-U Contract Account Status (VKONT-VKOSTATUS); Oracle CC&B Account Status; PUC Service Disconnection Rules

description: Classification of the utility account by customer segment. Drives rate eligibility, billing rules, and regulatory reporting. Residential covers single-family and multi-family dwellings; commercial covers businesses; industrial covers large-load manufacturing and processing facilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|industrial|municipal|agricultural|street_lighting|transportation|government — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IS-U Account Determination (VKTYP); Oracle CC&B Account Type; FERC Form 1 Customer Classification; EIA-861

description: Date and time when the account was first activated and service was energized. Represents the precise business event time of account creation and service commencement. Used for SLA tracking of new service connections.
reference: SAP IS-U Contract Account Creation; Oracle CC&B Account Activation; State PUC New Connect SLA

description: Indicates whether this account has an Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) smart meter installed, enabling automated interval reads, remote connect/disconnect, and real-time usage data. Drives eligibility for TOU rates, demand response programs, and enhanced billing analytics.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM; SAP IS-U Device Management; FERC Order 888; State PUC AMI Deployment Plans; IEEE C12.19

description: Indicates whether this account is enrolled in automatic payment (AutoPay), where the bill amount is automatically debited from the customer's bank account or charged to a payment card on the due date. Reduces collections risk and improves cash flow.
reference: SAP IS-U FI-CA Payment Method; Oracle CC&B Auto Pay; NAESB WEQ; PCI DSS

description: Code identifying the billing cycle (meter reading and bill generation schedule) assigned to this account. Determines the monthly read date and bill due date. Managed in SAP IS-U Billing Order or Oracle CC&B Bill Cycle. Examples: BC01 (1st of month), BC15 (15th of month).
reference: SAP IS-U Billing Order (ABRSPERR); Oracle CC&B Bill Cycle; State PUC Billing Rules

description: Indicates whether this account has Behind-the-Meter (BTM) generation or storage assets installed at the premise. BTM assets include solar PV, battery storage, and combined heat and power (CHP) systems that are not directly visible to the grid operator. Drives load disaggregation and DER management workflows.
reference: FERC Order 2222; State PUC DER Rules; DERMS Integration; IEEE 1547

description: Indicates whether this account is enrolled in a budget billing (levelized payment plan) program, where monthly bills are averaged over a 12-month period to smooth seasonal fluctuations. Affects bill calculation and settlement processes in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B.
reference: SAP IS-U Budget Billing (ABSCHLAG); Oracle CC&B Budget Billing; State PUC Tariff

description: The native primary key or account identifier as stored in the source CIS (SAP IS-U contract account number VKONT, or Oracle CC&B account ID). Enables traceability back to the operational system of record for reconciliation, audit, and issue resolution.
reference: SAP IS-U Contract Account (VKONT); Oracle CC&B Account ID; SOX Data Lineage; Enterprise MDM Policy

description: Identifies the operational Customer Information System (CIS) from which this account record originates. Critical for data lineage, deduplication, and cross-system reconciliation in multi-CIS utility environments. Values: SAP_IS_U (SAP Industry Solution for Utilities), ORACLE_CCB (Oracle Customer Care and Billing), LEGACY_CIS (decommissioned legacy system).
reference: SAP IS-U; Oracle CC&B; Enterprise Data Governance Policy; SOX Data Lineage

description: Date on which the service agreement or contract associated with this account is scheduled to expire or terminate. Null for open-ended tariff service. For fixed-term commercial or industrial contracts and PPAs, this date triggers renewal or renegotiation workflows.
reference: SAP IS-U Contract (VERTRAG); Oracle CC&B Service Agreement; FERC PPA; State PUC Tariff

description: Date on which the service agreement or contract associated with this account became effective. For residential accounts this typically aligns with move_in_date; for commercial and industrial accounts this may reflect a separately negotiated contract effective date, Power Purchase Agreement (PPA), or special contract term.
reference: SAP IS-U Contract (VERTRAG); Oracle CC&B Service Agreement; FERC PPA; State PUC Tariff

description: Timestamp when this account record was first created in the source CIS system. Represents the audit creation time for data lineage and SOX compliance. Distinct from activation_timestamp which represents the business event of service energization.
reference: SAP IS-U Contract Account Creation Timestamp; Oracle CC&B Account Created Date; SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 30

description: Credit risk classification assigned to the account based on payment history, credit bureau data, and internal scoring. Governs deposit requirements, payment plan eligibility, and collections treatment. Managed within SAP IS-U FICA or Oracle CC&B Collections module.
reference: SAP IS-U FI-CA Credit Management (FKKCR); Oracle CC&B Credit Review; State PUC Credit and Deposit Rules; SOX Internal Controls

description: Dollar amount of security deposit held by the utility for this account, as required by credit class or PUC tariff rules. Refundable upon account closure in good standing. Tracked in SAP IS-U FI-CA or Oracle CC&B.
reference: SAP IS-U FI-CA Security Deposit (FKKDEP); Oracle CC&B Deposit; State PUC Deposit Rules; SOX

business_glossary_term: Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging Rate Enrollment Flag
description: Indicates whether this account is enrolled in a special Electric Vehicle (EV) charging rate or managed charging program. Drives eligibility for off-peak EV charging incentives and supports grid load forecasting for EV adoption planning.
reference: State PUC EV Tariff; FERC Order 2222; DOE EV Infrastructure Programs; IEEE 2030.5

description: Date of the most recent bill generated for this account. Used to identify unbilled accounts, support billing cycle management, and calculate days since last bill for collections and customer service workflows.
reference: SAP IS-U Billing Document (ERDK); Oracle CC&B Bill; State PUC Billing Rules

description: Date of the most recent payment posted to this account. Used in collections scoring, credit class review, and customer service inquiries. Sourced from SAP IS-U FI-CA payment history or Oracle CC&B payment records.
reference: SAP IS-U FI-CA Payment (DFKKZP); Oracle CC&B Payment; State PUC Collections Rules

description: Timestamp of the most recent change to account_status. Enables tracking of the current status duration and supports collections and reconnection SLA monitoring. Complements the account status history table for current-state queries.
reference: SAP IS-U Contract Account Status History; Oracle CC&B Account Status History; State PUC Disconnection/Reconnection Rules

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this account record in the source CIS or Silver Layer. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail requirements for SOX and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP IS-U Change Document; Oracle CC&B Audit Log; SOX Internal Controls; GDPR Article 30

description: Indicates whether a customer at this account relies on electrically-powered life support equipment (e.g., home dialysis, ventilator, oxygen concentrator). Triggers mandatory advance disconnection notice requirements and priority restoration during outages per PUC regulations.
reference: State PUC Life Support Rules; NERC EOP-011; State Emergency Management Regulations

description: Date on which the customer initiated service at the associated premise, triggering account activation and the start of billing. Corresponds to the service start date in CIS. Critical for proration of first bill and regulatory reporting of new connects.
reference: SAP IS-U Move-In (EINZUG); Oracle CC&B Start Service; State PUC Service Rules

description: Date on which the customer terminated service at the associated premise, triggering final bill generation and account closure. Null for currently active accounts. Corresponds to the service end date in CIS.
reference: SAP IS-U Move-Out (AUSZUG); Oracle CC&B Stop Service; State PUC Service Rules

description: Current outstanding balance on the account as of the last billing or payment posting. Represents the net amount owed by the customer (positive) or credit due to the customer (negative). Sourced from SAP IS-U FI-CA or Oracle CC&B AR sub-ledger. Used for collections prioritization and credit risk management.
reference: SAP IS-U FI-CA (DFKKOP); Oracle CC&B Financial Transaction; SOX AR Controls; State PUC Collections Rules

description: Indicates whether this account is enrolled in paperless (electronic) billing, receiving bills via email or online portal rather than postal mail. Supports sustainability reporting and customer engagement analytics.
reference: Oracle CC&B Bill Delivery; SAP IS-U Bill Print Suppression; State PUC E-Bill Rules

description: Customer's preferred channel for receiving utility communications including bills, outage notifications, and program offers. Drives multi-channel engagement routing in CRM and outage notification systems (OMS). Aligns with customer consent preferences.
reference: Oracle CC&B Contact Preference; SAP CRM Interaction Channel; TCPA; GDPR Article 7

description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the customer's preferred language for all communications. Drives bill language selection, IVR routing, and outage notification language. Examples: en, es, zh-TW, vi, ko.
reference: ISO 639-1; ISO 3166-1; State PUC Language Access Rules; Title VI Civil Rights Act

description: Two-letter US state code (ISO 3166-2 subdivision) identifying the state Public Utility Commission (PUC) with regulatory authority over this account. Determines applicable tariff, consumer protection rules, and reporting obligations. Critical for multi-state utility operations.
reference: ISO 3166-2; State PUC Jurisdiction; FERC; NERC

description: Code identifying the voluntary renewable energy plan or green tariff program in which this account is enrolled. Examples include 100% renewable, green power option, or Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) purchase programs. Supports Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|green_100pct|green_50pct|rec_purchase|community_solar|wind_option — promote to reference product]
reference: State PUC RPS Rules; EPA Green Power Partnership; REC (Renewable Energy Certificate) Standards; NERC

description: Reason code explaining the most recent account status transition. Examples include non_payment, customer_request, move_out, returned_check, medical_baseline, regulatory_order. Supports collections analytics and regulatory compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_payment|customer_request|move_out|returned_check|medical_baseline|regulatory_order|fraud|deceased — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IS-U Disconnection Reason (ABGRU); Oracle CC&B Stop Reason; State PUC Disconnection Rules; NERC CIP

description: Indicates whether this account is enrolled on a Time-of-Use (TOU) rate schedule, where energy prices vary by time of day and season to reflect grid conditions. Requires AMI metering. Supports demand flexibility analytics and rate design evaluation.
reference: State PUC TOU Tariff; SAP IS-U Rate Type; Oracle CC&B Rate Schedule; FERC Order 2222

description: Unique identifier for the account-premise relationship record. Primary key for the account_premise_link entity.
Reference to the formal service agreement governing this account-premise relationship. Links to the contract or service agreement entity.
reference: SAP IS-U Contract Account, Oracle CC&B Service Agreement

description: Reference to the forwarding address for final bill delivery and correspondence after account-premise relationship termination. Used in move-out processing.
Reference to the customer account associated with this premise. Links to the account entity in the Customer Information System (CIS).
reference: SAP IS-U Business Partner, Oracle CC&B Account

description: Reference to the service order that initiated this account-premise relationship. Links to the move-in or service connection order.
reference: SAP IS-U Service Order, Oracle CC&B Field Activity

description: Reference to the service premise associated with this account. Links to the premise entity representing the physical service location.
reference: SAP IS-U Installation, Oracle CC&B Service Point

description: Reference to the tenant account when this link represents a landlord-of-record relationship. Used to track tenant accounts associated with a landlord premise. Null for non-landlord relationships.
FK to customer.premise.premise_id — The association entity must reference the premise it links. account_premise_link.premise_id → premise.premise_id.

Indicates whether this account has billing responsibility for the premise. In master-metered or landlord scenarios, multiple accounts may be linked to a premise but only one has billing responsibility.
Preferred communication channel for outage notifications and service alerts for this account-premise relationship. Used by OMS and customer engagement systems.
Security deposit amount collected for this account-premise relationship. Null if no deposit was required. Expressed in local currency.
Indicates whether a security deposit was required for this account-premise relationship based on credit classification and service territory regulations.
Indicates whether this account-premise relationship is enrolled in a Distributed Energy Resource (DER) program such as demand response, virtual power plant, or distributed generation program.
reference: FERC Order 2222, State PUC DER Programs

description: Indicates whether a final bill has been issued for this account-premise relationship upon termination. Used in move-out and service disconnection workflows.
Timestamp when this account-premise link record was first created in the Customer Information System (CIS). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Date when the account-premise relationship was terminated. Represents move-out date or service disconnection date. Null for active relationships.
Timestamp when this account-premise link record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking in customer relationship management.
Free-text notes and comments regarding this account-premise relationship. Used by customer service representatives to document special circumstances, arrangements, or historical context.
Date when the account-premise relationship became effective. Represents move-in date or service initiation date for the account at this premise.
Current lifecycle status of the account-premise relationship. Indicates whether the link is currently in effect or has been terminated.
Reason code for termination of the account-premise relationship. Provides business context for why the link was ended. Null for active relationships.
Classification of the relationship between the account and premise. Defines the role and responsibility of the account holder at the service location.
Indicates whether this relationship involves a master-metered configuration where a single meter serves multiple tenant accounts. Common in multi-family residential and commercial properties.

Indicates whether this account-premise relationship involves a Net Energy Metering (NEM) participant with Behind the Meter (BTM) generation. Used to identify customers with distributed energy resources.
reference: FERC Net Metering Policy, State PUC NEM Tariffs

description: Indicates whether the account holder has authorized receipt of outage notifications and service alerts for this premise. Used by Outage Management System (OMS) for customer communication.
Classification of the occupancy status for this account-premise relationship. Indicates whether the account holder is an owner, tenant, or if the premise is vacant.
Numeric ranking of this account-premise link when multiple accounts are associated with a single premise. Used to determine primary contact for outage notifications and field operations.
Reference to the property management company responsible for this premise when the link type is property manager. Used in commercial and multi-family property management scenarios.
Identifier of the operational system of record that originated this account-premise link. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.
Original primary key or unique identifier from the source system. Used for data reconciliation and bidirectional synchronization with operational systems.
Indicates whether vacancy billing is active for this account-premise relationship. When true, the landlord or property manager is billed for usage during tenant vacancy periods.
Unique identifier for the credit profile record. Primary key for the credit profile entity.
Reference to the customer account to which this credit profile applies. Links credit assessment to the billing account in CIS (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B).
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Contract Account; Oracle CC&B Account

description: FK to customer.account.account_id — Credit classification is assessed per account. credit_profile.account_id → account.account_id.
Average monthly payment amount over the past 12 months. Used for deposit calculation and payment arrangement sizing.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Payment History Analysis

description: Date the bankruptcy was discharged or dismissed. Credit profile may be eligible for upgrade after discharge and demonstration of payment performance.
reference: Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA); Bankruptcy Code

description: Date of the most recent bankruptcy filing. Used to determine if bankruptcy is still active and impacts credit classification duration.
reference: Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA); Bankruptcy Code

description: Indicates whether the customer has an active or recent bankruptcy filing. Impacts collections strategy and may trigger automatic deposit requirement per PUC rules.
reference: Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA); SAP IS-U FICA Bankruptcy Indicator

description: Number of payment arrangements that were broken (customer failed to make scheduled payments). Impacts future payment arrangement eligibility and credit classification.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Installment Plan Compliance; Oracle CC&B Payment Plan History

description: Current stage in the collections process for past-due balances. Drives automated dunning notices, disconnection scheduling, and external collections referral. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current|reminder|final_notice|disconnection_pending|disconnected|write_off|legal_action — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Collections Management; Oracle CC&B Collections Process

description: Timestamp when this credit profile record was first created in the CIS. Required for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SAP IS-U Audit Trail; Oracle CC&B Audit Log

description: Credit classification code assigned to the customer account based on payment history, credit score, and risk assessment. Determines deposit requirements and collection strategies.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Credit Management; Oracle CC&B Credit Configuration

description: Indicates whether the customer has placed a credit freeze with the credit bureaus, preventing external credit checks. Requires manual credit assessment process.
reference: Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA); Credit Freeze Regulations

description: Maximum outstanding balance allowed before service disconnection or additional deposit requirement is triggered. Based on credit class and deposit on file.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Credit Limit Configuration; State PUC Credit Rules

description: Current status of the credit profile record. Active profiles are used for deposit and collections decisions; under_review profiles are pending credit reassessment.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Credit Management; Oracle CC&B Credit Configuration

description: Date of the most recent credit review or reassessment. Credit profiles are typically reviewed annually or upon significant payment events (late payments, disconnection, bankruptcy).
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Credit Review Process; Oracle CC&B Credit Review

description: Credit score range band obtained from external credit bureau (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion). Used for initial credit classification and deposit determination.
reference: Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA); FICO Score Ranges

description: Security deposit amount required from the customer, typically calculated as 2-3 months of estimated billing. Null if no deposit is required.
reference: State PUC Deposit Calculation Rules; SAP IS-U FICA Deposit

description: Indicates whether a security deposit is required for this account based on credit classification and PUC regulations.
reference: State PUC Deposit Regulations; SAP IS-U Deposit Management

description: Type of security deposit instrument held for the account. Cash deposits are held in trust accounts; surety bonds and letters of credit are third-party guarantees.
reference: State PUC Deposit Regulations; SAP IS-U Deposit Types

description: Reason code for waiving the security deposit requirement. Regulatory exemptions include low-income program enrollment, medical necessity, and senior citizen status per PUC rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: excellent_credit|existing_customer|low_income_program|senior_citizen|medical_necessity|regulatory_exemption|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: State PUC Deposit Waiver Rules; Low-Income Program Regulations

description: Total number of service disconnections for non-payment in the account history. Indicates chronic payment issues and impacts credit classification.
reference: SAP IS-U Disconnection History; Oracle CC&B Service Orders

description: Date this credit profile became effective. Credit profiles are versioned; this date marks the start of this version's validity period.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Credit Management; Oracle CC&B Credit Configuration

description: Date this credit profile version expires or is superseded by a new version. Null for the current active credit profile.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Credit Management; Oracle CC&B Credit Configuration

description: Credit bureau used to obtain the credit score and credit report for this customer. Required for FCRA compliance and dispute resolution.
reference: Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA); Credit Bureau Integration

description: Date the external credit bureau check was performed. Credit checks are typically performed at account establishment and periodically for high-risk accounts.
reference: Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA); SAP IS-U Credit Bureau Integration

description: Highest past-due balance amount in the account history. Indicates maximum payment risk exposure and informs deposit amount calculation.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Receivables History; Oracle CC&B Aging Analysis

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this credit profile record. Tracks credit reassessments, manual overrides, and system updates.
reference: SAP IS-U Audit Trail; Oracle CC&B Audit Log

description: Number of late payments (payments received after due date) in the rolling 12-month period. Key metric for credit class downgrade and disconnection eligibility.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Payment History; Oracle CC&B Collections

description: Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in a low-income assistance program (LIHEAP, state-specific programs). Enrollment may waive deposit requirements and provide disconnection protections.
reference: LIHEAP Regulations; State PUC Low-Income Programs

description: User ID or name of the credit management staff member who performed the manual override. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; SAP IS-U User Audit Trail

description: Date the manual credit classification override was performed. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; SAP IS-U Audit Trail

description: Indicates whether the credit classification was manually overridden by credit management staff. Overrides require supervisor approval and documented justification.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Credit Management Workflow; Internal Credit Policy

description: Free-text justification for manual credit classification override. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.
reference: Internal Credit Policy; SOX Internal Controls

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic credit review. Typically set 12 months from the last review date or earlier if triggered by payment issues.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Credit Review Schedule

description: Total number of payment arrangements or installment plans established for this account. Excessive arrangements may indicate chronic payment issues and impact credit classification.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Installment Plan History; Oracle CC&B Payment Plans

description: Indicates whether the account is eligible for a payment arrangement or installment plan based on credit class, payment history, and PUC regulations.
reference: State PUC Payment Arrangement Rules; SAP IS-U FICA Installment Plans

description: Number of returned payments (NSF checks, ACH returns, declined credit cards) in the rolling 12-month period. Indicates payment reliability issues and may trigger deposit requirement.
reference: SAP IS-U FICA Payment Processing; Oracle CC&B Payment History

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer profile record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for all downstream joins across billing, metering, and demand response domains.
reference: SAP IS-U Business Partner; Oracle CC&B Person/Account

description: Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Profile has embedded mailing address fields that should be normalized to reference the address table. The profile table has mailing_address_line1, mailing_address_line2, mailing_city, mailing_state_pr

Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in automatic payment (AutoPay), authorizing the utility to automatically debit the customer's bank account or payment card on the bill due date. Reduces collections risk and improves cash flow predictability.
reference: NACHA ACH Rules; State PUC AutoPay Regulations; SAP IS-U FICA Payment Method; Oracle CC&B AutoPay

description: Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in a budget billing (levelized payment) program, where monthly charges are averaged over 12 months to smooth seasonal bill fluctuations. Drives levelized billing calculation in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B.
reference: State PUC Budget Billing Rules; SAP IS-U Budget Billing; Oracle CC&B Budget Billing

description: Legal structure of the commercial or industrial customer organization. Null for residential person customers. Used for credit assessment, tax treatment, and regulatory classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sole_proprietor|partnership|llc|corporation|non_profit|government|trust|cooperative — promote to reference product]
reference: IRS Entity Classification; State Corporation Commission; SAP IS-U BP Legal Form

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the customer in the source Customer Information System (CIS). Maps to SAP IS-U Business Partner (BP) number or Oracle CC&B Person ID. Used for cross-system reconciliation and customer-facing correspondence.
reference: SAP IS-U BP Master; Oracle CC&B Person

description: Customer's preferred channel for receiving utility communications including bills, outage notifications, demand response alerts, and program enrollment confirmations. Drives multi-channel engagement routing in CRM and OMS notification systems.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act); CAN-SPAM Act; SAP IS-U BP Communication Method; Oracle CC&B Contact Preference

description: Timestamp when the customer profile record was first created in the source CIS system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Represents the customer's initial enrollment date with the utility. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format per model conventions.
reference: SAP IS-U BP Creation Date; Oracle CC&B Person Created Date; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: Internal credit risk classification assigned to the customer based on payment history, credit bureau data, and utility-specific scoring. Drives deposit requirements, payment plan eligibility, and collections treatment. Sourced from SAP IS-U FICA credit segment or Oracle CC&B credit rating.
reference: FCRA (Fair Credit Reporting Act); State PUC Deposit Rules; SAP IS-U FICA Credit Segment; Oracle CC&B Credit Rating

description: Numeric credit score obtained from a third-party credit bureau or internal scoring model. Range typically 300–850 for FICO-based scores. Used for deposit waiver decisions, payment arrangement eligibility, and collections prioritization. Null if no credit history exists.
reference: FCRA (Fair Credit Reporting Act); FICO Score Standards; SAP IS-U FICA Credit Score

description: Primary market segment classification of the customer. Drives rate schedule eligibility, billing treatment, and regulatory reporting. Residential covers individual households; commercial covers small-to-medium businesses; industrial covers large-load manufacturing and processing facilities; agricultural covers farming operations; government covers public sector entities.
reference: FERC Form 1; State PUC Rate Schedule Definitions; SAP IS-U Customer Class

description: Indicates whether the customer is a natural person (individual) or a legal organization (business entity). Determines which identity attributes are applicable and governs data handling obligations under privacy regulations.
reference: GDPR Article 4; SAP IS-U BP Category (Person/Organization)

description: Date of birth of the residential customer individual. Used for identity verification, age-based eligibility programs (e.g., senior discount rates), and credit assessment. Null for organization customers. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format per model conventions.
reference: GDPR Article 9; CCPA §1798.140; SAP IS-U BP Date of Birth

description: Indicates whether the customer is actively enrolled in a Demand Response (DR) program, authorizing the utility or ISO/RTO to curtail or shift the customer's load during peak demand events. Drives DR dispatch eligibility in DERMS and EMS systems.
reference: FERC Order 745; NERC BAL-002; State PUC DR Program Rules; Oracle CC&B Program Enrollment

description: Indicates whether the customer is required to pay a security deposit based on credit classification, payment history, or state PUC rules. True triggers deposit collection workflow in SAP IS-U FICA or Oracle CC&B.
reference: State PUC Deposit Regulations; SAP IS-U FICA Deposit Management; Oracle CC&B Deposit

description: Indicates whether the customer has one or more Distributed Energy Resources (DER) — such as rooftop solar, battery storage, or EV chargers — enrolled in the utility's DERMS platform. Enables participation in virtual power plant (VPP) programs and demand response dispatch.
reference: FERC Order 2222; IEEE 1547; NERC BAL-002; DERMS Integration Standards

description: Indicates whether the customer has requested to be excluded from non-essential marketing and outreach communications. Does not suppress legally required notices (e.g., disconnection warnings, safety alerts). Enforced in CRM and outbound communication systems.
reference: TCPA; CAN-SPAM Act; GDPR Article 21 (Right to Object); CCPA §1798.120

description: Primary email address for the customer used for electronic bill delivery, outage notifications, demand response program communications, and account alerts. Sourced from SAP IS-U BP email or Oracle CC&B Person email.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140; CAN-SPAM Act; SAP IS-U BP Email

description: Given name of the residential customer individual. Null for commercial/industrial entity customers. Sourced from SAP IS-U BP first name or Oracle CC&B Person first name field.
reference: GDPR Article 4; SAP IS-U BP Name Fields

description: Masked representation of the government-issued identification number, retaining only the last four digits (e.g., ***-**-1234 for SSN). Full ID is stored only in the source CIS system under restricted access. Used for identity confirmation without exposing the full identifier.
reference: CCPA §1798.140; GDPR Article 9; PCI DSS Tokenization Guidance; SAP IS-U BP ID Number

description: Type of government-issued identification document provided by the customer for identity verification. SSN and ITIN apply to US individuals; EIN applies to business entities; passport and drivers_license apply to individuals. Paired with masked government_id_masked field.
reference: NERC CIP-004; CCPA §1798.140; GDPR Article 9; SAP IS-U BP ID Type

description: Current status of the customer's identity verification process. Verified indicates successful validation against government ID or third-party identity service; unverified indicates not yet validated; pending indicates verification in progress; failed indicates verification attempt was unsuccessful; waived indicates verification requirement was formally waived (e.g., for government accounts).
reference: NERC CIP-004; FERC Standards of Conduct; SAP IS-U BP Verification Status

description: Date on which the customer's identity was most recently successfully verified. Used for compliance audit trails and periodic re-verification scheduling. Null if identity_verification_status is unverified or pending.
reference: NERC CIP-004; GDPR Article 5(1)(f); SAP IS-U BP Verification Date

description: Income tier classification assigned to the customer based on self-reported or verified household income relative to the federal poverty level (FPL). Used for low-income rate program eligibility, LIHEAP enrollment, and CARE/FERA program qualification. Tier 1 represents lowest income bracket.
reference: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program); State PUC CARE/FERA Program Rules; FERC Low-Income Provisions

description: Customer's preferred language for all communications, bills, and notices. Stored as ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., en, es, zh, fr, vi). Drives multi-language bill rendering and outage notification language selection in CIS and OMS systems.
reference: ISO 639-1; State PUC Language Access Requirements; SAP IS-U BP Language

description: Family name of the residential customer individual. Null for commercial/industrial entity customers. Sourced from SAP IS-U BP last name or Oracle CC&B Person last name field.
reference: GDPR Article 4; SAP IS-U BP Name Fields

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the customer profile record in the source CIS system. Used for change data capture (CDC) processing in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline and audit trail compliance.
reference: SAP IS-U BP Change Date; Oracle CC&B Person Last Modified; GDPR Article 5(1)(f)

description: Full legal name of the customer as registered with the utility. For residential customers this is the individual's full name; for commercial/industrial customers this is the registered business entity name. Used for billing, regulatory correspondence, and identity verification.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140; SAP IS-U BP Name Fields

description: Current state of the customer profile in its lifecycle. Active indicates a current utility customer; inactive indicates a former customer with no active service agreements; pending indicates a new customer application in progress; suspended indicates temporary hold due to credit or compliance issues; deceased indicates confirmed death of a residential customer; merged indicates the profile was consolidated into another record.
reference: SAP IS-U BP Status; Oracle CC&B Person Status; State PUC Customer Service Rules

description: Indicates whether the customer has been determined eligible for low-income energy assistance programs such as CARE (California Alternate Rates for Energy), FERA, LIHEAP, or equivalent state-specific programs. Drives discounted rate application in billing.
reference: LIHEAP Federal Program; State PUC CARE/FERA Rules; DOE Weatherization Assistance Program; Oracle CC&B Program Enrollment

description: Code identifying the specific low-income assistance program the customer is enrolled in (e.g., CARE, FERA, LIHEAP, HEAP, REACH). Null if low_income_assistance_eligible is false. Used for rate discount application and regulatory reporting to state PUC.
reference: State PUC Low-Income Program Definitions; LIHEAP; DOE Weatherization Assistance Program

description: Indicates whether the customer has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications about energy efficiency programs, new rate plans, and utility products. Required under GDPR opt-in rules for EU-adjacent operations and CCPA for California customers.
reference: GDPR Article 6(1)(a); CCPA §1798.120; TCPA; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Indicates whether the customer qualifies for a medical baseline allowance due to a life-threatening illness or condition requiring life-support equipment. Triggers additional baseline energy allocation at the lowest rate tier and special disconnection protections under state PUC rules.
reference: State PUC Medical Baseline Rules; CPUC Rule 11; Oracle CC&B Medical Baseline Program

description: Mobile telephone number for the customer, used specifically for SMS-based outage alerts, two-factor authentication, and demand response text notifications. Distinct from primary phone which may be a landline. Stored in E.164 format.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140; TCPA; SAP IS-U BP Mobile Number

description: Indicates whether the customer participates in a Net Energy Metering (NEM) program, meaning they have behind-the-meter (BTM) generation (typically solar PV) and receive credits for excess energy exported to the grid. Drives bidirectional metering requirements and NEM tariff application.
reference: FERC Order 2222; State PUC NEM Tariff Rules; PURPA; SAP IS-U NEM Rate Category

description: Indicates whether the customer has opted into electronic (paperless) bill delivery, suppressing physical mail generation. Drives bill rendering workflow in SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B billing engine.

description: Primary contact telephone number for the customer. Used for outage notifications, payment reminders, demand response alerts, and field crew coordination. Stored in E.164 international format.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140; TCPA; SAP IS-U BP Phone Number

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this customer profile was sourced. Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation.

description: Federal tax identification number for commercial/industrial customers (EIN) or individual taxpayer identification for sole proprietors. Used for 1099 reporting, tax exemption validation, and regulatory filings. Masked in analytics layer; full value retained in source CIS.
reference: IRS Publication 1635; SOX Section 302; SAP S/4HANA Tax Number

description: Indicates whether the customer has consented to sharing their energy usage and profile data with authorized third parties (e.g., solar installers, EV charging providers, energy management platforms) under Green Button Connect My Data or similar data access frameworks.
reference: GDPR Article 6; CCPA §1798.100; Green Button Connect My Data Standard; NAESB REQ.21

Current number of active customer accounts assigned to this segment. Updated periodically from CIS systems for segment size tracking and program capacity planning.

description: Indicates whether this segment exhibits significant seasonal consumption variation (e.g., residential cooling load in summer, agricultural irrigation in growing season). Used for seasonal rate design and forecasting adjustments.

description: Detailed business description of the segment characteristics, typical customer profiles, usage patterns, and program applicability. Provides context for business users and supports segment definition documentation.

Current lifecycle status of the segment classification. Active segments are available for customer assignment; pending segments await regulatory approval; retired segments are maintained for historical reporting only.

Primary market segmentation tier defining the customer class and usage profile. description: Indicates whether the property owner or landlord must be notified of the service change per tariff rules or regulatory requirements. Relevant for rental properties where the landlord has a standing notification agreement with the utility.

description: The date on which the landlord or property owner was notified of the service change associated with this move order. Populated only when landlord_notification_required is true. Used for regulatory compliance documentation.

description: Indicates whether the customer has an approved medical baseline allowance (life support or medical necessity designation) that requires special handling during service disconnection or move-out. When true, additional notification and regulatory compliance steps are required before service termination.

description: The date on which the meter was physically or remotely read in connection with this move order. Used as the billing cut-off date for the final bill (move-out) or the start-of-service read date (move-in). Sourced from Oracle Utilities MDM or SAP IS-U Device Management.
reference: SAP IS-U Device Management; Oracle Utilities MDM; State PUC metering standards

description: Indicates whether the customer associated with this move order is enrolled in a Net Energy Metering (NEM) program, typically due to rooftop solar or other behind-the-meter (BTM) generation. description: Externally-known business identifier for the move order, assigned by the source system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Used for customer-facing correspondence and cross-system reconciliation.

description: Current lifecycle state of the move order workflow. Pending: submitted but not yet processed. In_progress: actively being executed (meter read scheduled, field crew dispatched). Completed: service change fully effected. Cancelled: withdrawn before execution. Rejected: failed validation or business rules.
reference: SAP IS-U Move-In/Move-Out; Oracle CC&B Start/Stop Service

description: Classifies the nature of the service order: move_in (new service activation at a premise), move_out (service termination at a premise), transfer (simultaneous move-out and move-in for a change of occupant), reconnect (restoration of previously disconnected service), or disconnect (non-payment or voluntary disconnection). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: move_in|move_out|transfer|reconnect|disconnect — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: SAP IS-U EVER; Oracle CC&B Start/Stop Service; NAESB Retail Electric Provider standards

description: The tariff rate schedule code assigned to the new service account upon move-in or transfer. Determines the billing rate class (e.g., residential, small commercial, large industrial, TOU, CPP). Sourced from the applicable PUC-approved tariff.
reference: SAP IS-U Rate Management; Oracle CC&B Rates; State PUC tariff filings; FERC OATT

description: Indicates how the move-order meter read was obtained: actual (field technician physical read), estimated (calculated based on usage history when actual read is unavailable), AMI_remote (automated read via Advanced Metering Infrastructure), or customer_self_read (customer-reported reading).

description: The unit of measure for the final_read_value. Typically kWh for electric meters, but may be therms, CCF, or MCF for gas service points. Ensures correct interpretation of the meter read value across commodity types.
reference: SAP IS-U Device Management; Oracle Utilities MDM; ANSI C12.20; NAESB standards

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or exceptions associated with this move order. May include field crew instructions, customer-specific handling notes, or escalation details entered by customer service representatives.
reference: SAP IS-U Move-In/Move-Out; Oracle CC&B Start/Stop Service

description: The date the customer requested the service change to take effect. This is the customer-stated desired effective date, which may differ from the actual effective date after scheduling and field execution.

description: The date on which the meter read associated with this move order is scheduled to be performed. May differ from meter_read_date (actual read date) if the read is rescheduled or delayed. Used for field crew scheduling and MDM read request management.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM; SAP IS-U Device Management; State PUC metering standards

description: Customer service classification at the time of the move order, determining applicable tariff, billing rules, and regulatory reporting category. Residential, small commercial, large commercial, industrial, agricultural, or lighting.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this move order record was sourced. SAP_IS-U: SAP Industry Solution for Utilities. Oracle_CCB: Oracle Utilities Customer Care and Billing. Manual: entered directly into the data platform. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

description: The native primary key or order identifier from the originating operational system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Enables traceability back to the source system record for audit, reconciliation, and issue resolution.

description: The date and time when the move order was first submitted into the CIS system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Represents the principal business event timestamp for order creation. Used for SLA tracking and order aging analysis.

description: Foreign key linking to forecast.zone. Business justification: Premises must be mapped to forecast zones for distribution load planning, capacity requirement allocation, outage impact analysis, and DER penetration forecasting. Essential for translating zone-level

Identifier of the meter reading route to which the premise is assigned for field meter reading operations. Used for route optimization, meter reader scheduling, and AMI walk-by/drive-by reading coordination in Oracle Utilities MDM.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Meter Reading Route; SAP IS-U Meter Reading Order

description: Foreign key linking to distribution.network_segment. Business justification: Premises are physically connected to a specific network segment, essential for outage impact scoping, hosting capacity assessment for DER interconnection, and FLISR topology modeling. Distribution eng

Foreign key linking to transmission.pnode. Business justification: Transmission-connected premises (served at high voltage) are mapped to a pnode for load forecasting, LMP settlement, and demand response dispatch in RTO/ISO markets. Load research and grid planning re

Foreign key linking to distribution.service_territory. Business justification: Premise-level service territory assignment is foundational for reliability reporting (SAIDI/SAIFI), outage management zone routing, and rate schedule eligibility. Distribution planners and regulators

Indicates whether the premise is eligible for Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) smart meter deployment based on infrastructure readiness, service voltage, and utility AMI rollout program criteria. Drives meter deployment prioritization in Oracle Utilities MDM.

reference: SAP IS-U Billing Cycle; Oracle CC&B Bill Cycle; State PUC Billing Frequency Rules

description: Detailed structural classification of the premise beyond the high-level premise type. Used for load profile modeling, infrastructure planning, and demand response program eligibility. Examples: single-family, multi-family, high-rise, substation-fed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_family|multi_family|high_rise|substation_fed|mobile_home|commercial_strip|warehouse|manufacturing|farm — promote to reference product]

description: Climate zone designation assigned to the premise based on geographic location and DOE/ASHRAE climate classification. description: Timestamp when the premise record was first created in the source CIS system (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Provides audit trail for data governance, regulatory compliance, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: Indicates whether the premise has an active Distributed Energy Resource (DER) enrollment, including rooftop solar, battery storage, or other behind-the-meter (BTM) generation assets registered in the utility's Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS). description: Indicates whether the premise has a registered electric vehicle (EV) charging installation. Used for distribution load forecasting, EV rate schedule eligibility, demand response program targeting, and grid modernization planning.

description: Total conditioned floor area of the premise structure in square feet. Used for energy intensity benchmarking (kWh per square foot), energy efficiency program eligibility, demand response capacity estimation, and load profile normalization.

description: Assessor's parcel number or GIS parcel identifier linking the premise to the county tax assessor's spatial parcel layer in Esri ArcGIS Utility Network. Enables land-use analysis, property boundary correlation, and infrastructure planning.

description: Date of the most recent utility field inspection of the premise's service entrance, metering equipment, or electrical infrastructure. Used for compliance tracking, safety program management, and asset maintenance scheduling in Ventyx Asset Suite.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the premise record in the source CIS system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance per SOX and NERC CIP requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls; NERC CIP-007; SAP IS-U Change Document; Oracle CC&B Audit Log

description: Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the physical service location as recorded in the utility's Geographic Information System (GIS). Used for outage management spatial analysis, field crew routing, and distribution network modeling in Esri ArcGIS Utility Network.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; WGS84 Coordinate Reference System; IEEE 1547 Interconnection Standards

description: Indicates whether a registered life-support equipment user resides at the premise. Triggers disconnection protection, priority outage notification, and special field crew protocols per state PUC regulations. Distinct from medical_baseline_flag which governs billing treatment.

description: Current operational state of the premise within the utility's service portfolio. Drives eligibility for new service connections, billing activation, and outage management inclusion. Active premises are energized and receiving service; decommissioned premises have been permanently removed from service.
reference: SAP IS-U Premise Status; Oracle CC&B Premise Status

description: Utility-assigned load profile class code used to model the premise's expected energy consumption pattern for billing, forecasting, and settlement purposes. Applied to non-interval-metered customers for load research and wholesale market settlement. Sourced from Oracle Utilities MDM or SAP IS-U.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Load Profile; NERC Load Forecasting Standards; FERC Settlement Procedures

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the physical service location as recorded in the utility's Geographic Information System (GIS). Used in conjunction with latitude for spatial analysis, outage polygon mapping, and DER interconnection planning.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Utility Network; WGS84 Coordinate Reference System; IEEE 1547 Interconnection Standards

description: Indicates whether the premise has a registered medical baseline allowance due to a life-support or medical necessity condition. Triggers special billing treatment, outage notification priority, and disconnection protection protocols per state PUC regulations.
reference: State PUC Medical Baseline Tariff Rules; CPUC Rule 11 (California); Oracle CC&B Medical Baseline Configuration

description: Number of meter sockets (meter points) installed at the premise. Multi-socket premises include multi-family dwellings, commercial complexes, and industrial facilities with multiple service points. Drives meter deployment planning in Oracle Utilities MDM and SAP IS-U Device Management.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM; SAP IS-U Device Management; ANSI C12.1 Electric Meters Standards

description: Indicates whether the premise is eligible for Net Energy Metering (NEM) based on service territory rules, interconnection capacity, and applicable state PUC tariff provisions. Supports Distributed Energy Resource (DER) enrollment and behind-the-meter (BTM) generation tracking.
reference: State PUC NEM Tariff Rules; FERC PURPA; IEEE 1547 Interconnection Standard; SAP IS-U NEM Configuration

description: Number of individual dwelling or tenant units within the premise. Applicable to multi-family, high-rise, and mixed-use premises. Used for per-unit load analysis, master-metered billing allocation, and demand response program enrollment capacity.
reference: Oracle CC&B Multi-Unit Premise Configuration; SAP IS-U Premise Unit Count

description: Recorded or estimated peak electrical demand in kilowatts (kW) for the premise. Used for transformer sizing, distribution capacity planning, demand charge billing, and demand response (DR) program baseline establishment. Sourced from Oracle Utilities MDM interval data or SAP IS-U demand registers.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM; SAP IS-U Demand Register; NERC Load Forecasting; State PUC Demand Charge Tariffs

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the premise as assigned by the utility's CIS (SAP IS-U or Oracle CC&B). Used in field operations, customer correspondence, and cross-system integration. Corresponds to SAP IS-U Premise Number or Oracle CC&B Premise ID.
reference: SAP IS-U Premise Number; Oracle Utilities CC&B Premise Identifier

description: High-level classification of the premise by customer segment and land use. Drives rate schedule eligibility, load profile assignment, and regulatory reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|industrial|agricultural|municipal|mixed_use — promote to reference product]
reference: FERC Form 1; EIA-861 Customer Class Definitions; SAP IS-U Premise Category

description: Date on which utility service was permanently terminated at the premise (e.g., demolition, decommissioning). Null for active premises. Used for premise lifecycle management and historical reporting.
reference: SAP IS-U Connection Object; Oracle CC&B Premise Deactivation; State PUC Service Termination Rules

description: Date on which utility service was first activated at the premise. Establishes the premise's service history baseline for billing, regulatory reporting, and infrastructure age analysis.
reference: SAP IS-U Connection Object; Oracle CC&B Premise Service Start; State PUC Service Commencement Rules

description: Nominal voltage class at which electric service is delivered to the premise. Determines applicable tariff rate schedule, metering configuration, and interconnection requirements. reference: FERC OATT; State PUC Rate Schedules; IEEE C84.1 Voltage Standards; ANSI C12.1

description: Indicates whether the premise qualifies for Time of Use (TOU) or Critical Peak Pricing (CPP) rate schedules based on metering capability (AMI), service class, and applicable PUC tariff provisions. Drives rate schedule assignment and customer enrollment workflows.

description: Unique identifier for the demand response aggregator or curtailment service provider (CSP). Primary key for the aggregator master record.

Name or description of the technology platform or system used by the aggregator to manage and dispatch aggregated customer load.
Short business code or abbreviation used internally to identify the aggregator in operational systems and reports.
Full legal name of the third-party demand response aggregator or curtailment service provider as registered with regulatory authorities.
Current operational status of the aggregator relationship with the utility, indicating whether the aggregator is authorized to aggregate customer load.
Classification of the aggregator based on its primary business model and service offering in the demand response ecosystem.

Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the aggregator relationship and operations.
Standard payment terms for incentive payments and settlements with the aggregator, such as net-30 or net-60 days.
Dollar value of the performance bond or financial guarantee posted by the aggregator to ensure compliance with contractual capacity commitments and performance obligations.

Financial penalty rate applied per MW of underperformance when the aggregator fails to deliver committed demand response capacity during an event.
Frequency at which demand response performance settlements and incentive payments are calculated and paid to the aggregator.
Federal tax identification number (EIN or TIN) for the aggregator legal entity, used for financial reporting and tax compliance.
Frequency in minutes at which telemetry data must be transmitted from aggregated customer sites to the utility or ISO/RTO (e.g., 5-minute intervals).
Indicates whether real-time telemetry data from aggregated customer sites is required for dispatch and settlement purposes.
Unique identifier for the curtailment measurement record. Primary key for post-event measurement records capturing actual load reduction achieved during a Demand Response (DR) event interval.

Reference to the parent DR event during which this curtailment was measured. Links to the DR event dispatch record that triggered the load reduction.

Reference to the meter or metering point from which interval data was sourced for this measurement. May be an AMI meter, SCADA point, or aggregated virtual meter.

Foreign key linking to metering.vee_rule. Business justification: curtailment_measurement.vee_flag and vee_method_code indicate VEE validation is applied to curtailment data. Linking to the specific vee_rule applied supports regulatory M&V audit trails, ISO/RTO sett
Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the baseline to account for day-of conditions (weather, operational changes). Typically ranges from 0.8 to 1.2. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.
Calculated baseline power demand in kilowatts (kW) representing what the resource would have demanded absent the DR event. Used as the reference for curtailment calculation.

Calculated baseline energy consumption in kilowatt-hours (kWh) representing what the resource would have consumed absent the DR event. Calculated using the baseline methodology specified in the DR program rules.

Code identifying the baseline calculation methodology applied for this measurement. Common methods include High X of Y (e.g., average of highest 3 of last 10 similar days), Mid X of Y, regression-based, control group, meter-before, or customer-provided baseline.

The bid quantity in kilowatts (kW) submitted by the resource or aggregator for this DR event interval. May differ from enrolled_capacity_kw if the resource bid a different amount for this specific event.
Timestamp when this curtailment measurement record was first created in the system. Represents the initial calculation or data ingestion time.

Calculated load curtailment achieved during the interval in kilowatts (kW). Computed as baseline_demand_kw minus metered_demand_kw. Positive values indicate successful load reduction; negative values indicate load increase.
Calculated energy curtailment achieved during the interval in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Computed as baseline_load_kwh minus metered_load_kwh. Used for settlement and incentive payment calculation.

Code indicating the quality status of the metered data. ACTUAL indicates raw meter data; ESTIMATED indicates VEE-filled gaps; VALIDATED indicates passed quality checks; SUSPECT indicates potential data issues; MISSING indicates no data received; REJECTED indicates failed validation.

Code identifying the source of the metered load data. Values include AMI (Advanced Metering Infrastructure), SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition), MANUAL (manual meter read), ESTIMATED (VEE-estimated), AGGREGATED (sum of multiple meters), or TELEMETRY (real-time sensor data).
The enrolled or nominated load reduction capacity in kilowatts (kW) for this resource during the DR event. Represents the committed curtailment target used for performance evaluation and settlement.

Calculated incentive payment amount in currency for this measurement interval. Computed as settlement_quantity_kw multiplied by incentive_rate_per_kwh and interval duration. Subject to program caps and adjustments.
The incentive payment rate in currency per kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) applicable to this measurement interval. Rate may vary by time-of-use period, event type, or performance tier.
Duration of the measurement interval in minutes. Common values are 5, 15, or 60 minutes depending on ISO/RTO market settlement rules and meter data granularity.
End timestamp of the measurement interval during the DR event. Defines the boundary of the measurement period for load curtailment calculation.
Start timestamp of the measurement interval during the DR event. Typically aligned to 5-minute, 15-minute, or hourly intervals per ISO/RTO settlement rules.

business_glossary_term: Independent System Operator (ISO) / Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) Market Code
description: Code identifying the ISO/RTO wholesale market to which this curtailment measurement is reported for settlement. Values include CAISO, ERCOT, ISONE, MISO, NYISO, PJM, SPP, or NONE for retail-only programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|ISONE|MISO|NYISO|PJM|SPP|NONE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The Locational Marginal Price in currency per megawatt-hour ($/MWh) at the resource location during this interval. Used to value the curtailment for wholesale market settlement or avoided cost calculation.

Lifecycle status of the curtailment measurement record. PRELIMINARY indicates initial calculation; VALIDATED indicates passed M&V review; SETTLED indicates used for payment calculation; DISPUTED indicates under review; ADJUSTED indicates corrected after dispute; FINAL indicates locked for regulatory reporting.
Actual power demand measured during the interval in kilowatts (kW). Represents the average or peak demand during the measurement period depending on metering configuration.
Actual energy consumption measured during the interval in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Sourced from AMI meter data, SCADA telemetry, or estimated via Validation Estimation and Editing (VEE) process.

Code identifying the Measurement and Verification (M&V) protocol applied to validate the curtailment calculation. Common protocols include IPMVP Options A-D (International Performance Measurement and Verification Protocol) or utility-specific custom methods.

Reference to the specific DR event for which this baseline is applicable. Null if this is a standing baseline not tied to a specific event.

Reference to the Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) meter device used to capture interval data for baseline calculation.
