Reference to the baseline record that supersedes this one. Null if this is the current active baseline. Used to maintain baseline version history.

Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the baseline to account for day-of conditions (e.g., weather, operational changes). Typically ranges from 0.8 to 1.2. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.

The method used to adjust the baseline for day-of conditions. Options include none (no adjustment), additive (add/subtract a fixed amount), multiplicative (apply a scaling factor), weather-normalized (adjust based on weather data), or pre-event-hours (adjust based on load in hours immediately before the event).
Timestamp when this baseline was approved for use. Required for regulatory audit trail and compliance reporting.
Username or identifier of the person who approved this baseline for use in M&V calculations. Required for regulatory audit trail.
The date on which this baseline load profile was calculated. Used for version control and audit trail purposes.
The date from which this baseline load profile becomes effective for Measurement and Verification (M&V) purposes.
The date on which this baseline load profile expires and is no longer valid for M&V calculations. Null if the baseline remains valid until superseded.

JSON-encoded array of baseline load values (kWh) for each interval within the event window. Each element represents the expected load for a specific time interval. Format: [{"interval_start":"2024-07-15T14:00:00","load_kwh":125.4},{"interval_start":"2024-07-15T14:15:00","load_kwh":128.2}].

The methodology used to calculate the customer baseline load. Common methods include 10-of-10 (average of 10 most recent similar days), high-10-of-10 (highest 10 days), mid-8-of-10 (middle 8 of 10), regression-based, weather-adjusted, or customer-specific algorithms approved by the ISO/RTO or PUC.
reference: NAESB WEQ-012 Demand Response Baseline Standards

description: Comma-separated list of dates (YYYY-MM-DD) used as source data for calculating this baseline. Provides traceability to the historical load data used in the calculation.
Current lifecycle status of the baseline record. Indicates whether the baseline is in draft, pending approval, approved for use, actively in use, superseded by a newer version, or rejected.
Version number of this baseline calculation. Increments with each recalculation or adjustment. Used for audit trail and historical comparison.
Name and version of the software system or calculation engine used to compute this baseline (e.g., Oracle MDM Baseline Calculator v3.2, Custom Python Engine v1.5). Used for audit and reproducibility.
Timestamp when this baseline record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Quality score (0-100) indicating the reliability of the input meter data used for baseline calculation. Based on completeness, accuracy, and validation status of interval data.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM VEE (Validation Estimation and Editing)

description: Method used to estimate missing interval data in the baseline calculation. Options include none (no estimation), linear interpolation, similar-day substitution, regression-based estimation, or carry-forward from previous interval.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM VEE Estimation Rules

description: The end timestamp of the DR event window for which this baseline applies. Defines the conclusion of the curtailment measurement period.
The start timestamp of the DR event window for which this baseline applies. Defines the beginning of the curtailment measurement period.
Comma-separated list of dates (YYYY-MM-DD) that were excluded from the baseline calculation due to holidays, prior DR events, or anomalous load conditions.
The duration of each interval in the baseline load profile, typically 15 or 60 minutes. Aligns with AMI meter reading intervals.
reference: Oracle Utilities MDM Interval Data Standards

business_glossary_term: Independent System Operator (ISO) / Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) Submission Timestamp
description: Timestamp when this baseline was submitted to the ISO/RTO for settlement. Null if not yet submitted.

business_glossary_term: Independent System Operator (ISO) / Regional Transmission Organization (RTO) Submitted Flag
description: Indicates whether this baseline has been submitted to the ISO/RTO for settlement purposes. True if submitted, False otherwise.
Username or identifier of the user or system process that last modified this baseline record. Required for audit trail.
Timestamp when this baseline record was last modified. Updated whenever any field in the record changes.
Number of intervals with missing or invalid meter data that were estimated or excluded from the baseline calculation. Used to assess baseline reliability.
Free-text notes documenting special circumstances, manual adjustments, or exceptions applied during baseline calculation. Used for audit trail and operational transparency.
The maximum demand (kW) observed in the baseline load profile during the event window. Used for capacity-based DR program settlement.
Indicates whether this baseline is included in PUC program performance reporting. True if included, False otherwise.
reference: State PUC Demand Response Reporting Requirements

description: Indicates whether this customer's load is temperature-sensitive and requires weather-adjusted baseline methodology. True if temperature-sensitive, False otherwise.

The sum of all interval baseline load values across the entire event window, expressed in kilowatt-hours (kWh). Used for aggregate curtailment measurement.
Identifier of the weather station used for weather-adjusted baseline calculations. Links to external weather data sources (e.g., NOAA, local meteorological services).
Username or identifier of the user or system process that created this baseline record. Required for audit trail.

Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was cancelled before completion. Null if the dispatch was not cancelled.
Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was completed and DER resources returned to normal operation. Marks the end of the dispatch lifecycle.

description: Indicates whether the VPP dispatch met the minimum performance requirements for the contracted service type. True if performance ratio exceeded the compliance threshold; false otherwise. Used for capacity accreditation and penalty assessment.

Number of individual DER assets (batteries, electric vehicles, smart inverters, controllable loads) that participated in this VPP dispatch instruction.
Percentage of DER assets in the VPP portfolio that successfully responded to the dispatch instruction, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.92 for 92% response rate). Used to assess portfolio reliability.

business_glossary_term: Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS) Integration Reference
description: External reference identifier linking this VPP dispatch to the corresponding instruction or transaction in the Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS). Enables traceability between utility dispatch systems and DER control platforms.
Total duration of the VPP dispatch instruction in minutes. Calculated as the difference between dispatch end time and dispatch start time.
Scheduled date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction expires and DER resources should return to normal operation.
reference: NAESB WEQ-015 - Wholesale Electric Quadrant Standards

description: Business identifier for the VPP dispatch instruction, formatted as VPP- followed by 10 digits. Used for external communication with ISO/RTO and aggregators.
reference: NAESB WEQ-015 - Wholesale Electric Quadrant Standards

description: Priority level of the VPP dispatch instruction. Emergency dispatches override normal operations; high priority dispatches support critical grid conditions; normal and low priority dispatches support economic optimization.

Business justification or operational reason for issuing the VPP dispatch instruction. Examples include peak demand management, renewable integration, frequency response, voltage support, or market arbitrage.

Originating system or entity that initiated the VPP dispatch instruction. Distinguishes between utility Energy Management System (EMS), ISO/RTO market dispatch, aggregator platform, Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS), or manual operator dispatch.
Scheduled date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction becomes effective and DER resources should begin responding.
reference: NAESB WEQ-015 - Wholesale Electric Quadrant Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the VPP dispatch instruction. Tracks progression from issuance through execution to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|issued|acknowledged|active|completed|cancelled|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date and time when the VPP dispatch instruction was issued to the aggregated DER portfolio. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.

description: Code identifying the ISO or RTO market region where the VPP dispatch is being executed. Examples include CAISO, ERCOT, MISO, PJM, NYISO, ISO-NE, and SPP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAISO|ERCOT|MISO|PJM|NYISO|ISO-NE|SPP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Locational Marginal Price for energy at the pricing node during the dispatch interval, expressed in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh). Used for settlement and revenue calculation.

Specific wholesale market product or ancillary service that the VPP dispatch is fulfilling. Examples include Day-Ahead Market (DAM) energy, Real-Time Market (RTM) energy, regulation up/down, or operating reserves. Maps to ISO/RTO product definitions.
Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about the VPP dispatch. Used to capture non-standard conditions or operator observations.
Ratio of actual MW response to target MW dispatch, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.95 for 95% performance). Used to assess VPP portfolio compliance and reliability for capacity accreditation.
Locational Marginal Price (LMP) node or pricing point where the VPP portfolio is registered and settled in the wholesale market. Used for financial settlement and locational value calculation.
Required rate of change in power output in megawatts per minute (MW/min). Specifies how quickly the VPP portfolio must transition from current state to target dispatch level.
reference: NERC BAL-001 - Real Power Balancing Control Performance

description: Type of grid service requested from the VPP portfolio. Includes energy arbitrage, capacity provision, frequency regulation, spinning reserve, non-spinning reserve, and voltage support. Determines the operational mode and response requirements for the DER fleet.

Total financial settlement amount in dollars for the VPP dispatch performance. Calculated based on actual MW response, LMP energy price, and applicable market product rates. Used for aggregator and customer incentive payments.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount. Typically USD for North American energy markets.
Time granularity for measuring and settling the VPP dispatch performance. Aligns with ISO/RTO market settlement intervals (typically 5-minute, 15-minute, or hourly).
Target power output or curtailment in megawatts (MW) requested from the VPP portfolio. Positive values indicate generation or discharge; negative values indicate load reduction or charging.
reference: NERC MOD-032 - Data for Power System Modeling

description: Frequency in seconds at which real-time telemetry data was collected from the VPP portfolio during the dispatch. Typical values range from 1 to 60 seconds depending on service type and ISO/RTO requirements.
Date and time when this VPP dispatch record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

description: Total annual capacity payment in USD for this registration, calculated as registered_capacity_mw × capacity_payment_rate_per_mw_day × days_in_season. Used for revenue forecasting and financial reporting.
Timestamp when this registration was approved by the ISO/RTO or utility. Marks the transition to certified status.

The ISO/RTO-approved baseline methodology used for measuring demand reduction performance (e.g., High 5 of 10, Mid 8 of 10, Regression-based). Determines how capacity performance is calculated during events.

Penalty rate in dollars per megawatt-hour ($/MWh) applied for non-performance during ISO/RTO performance assessment hours. Varies by market and performance tier.

Performance classification tier assigned by the ISO/RTO based on historical performance, resource type, and telemetry capabilities (e.g., Tier 1, Tier 2, Emergency-Only). Affects capacity credit and payment rates.

JSON structure containing detailed composition of the DR portfolio, including individual customer IDs, premise IDs, meter IDs, resource types, and individual capacity contributions. Used for portfolio management and settlement allocation.
Business name or designation for the DR capacity portfolio. May represent an aggregator portfolio, utility program portfolio, or Virtual Power Plant (VPP) resource pool.
Indicates whether this registration must be included in state Public Utility Commission (PUC) demand response program reporting and Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance filings.
Total registered demand response capacity in megawatts (MW) committed to the ISO/RTO capacity market. This is the capacity obligation that must be available during performance periods.
Date on which this DR capacity registration becomes effective and capacity obligations begin. Typically aligns with the start of the capacity delivery season.
Date on which this DR capacity registration expires and capacity obligations end. Typically aligns with the end of the capacity delivery season.
Current lifecycle status of the DR capacity registration. Active status is required for capacity payments and dispatch eligibility.
Classification of the DR capacity registration structure. Determines reporting obligations, settlement processes, and performance measurement methodologies.

Comma-separated list or JSON structure describing the mix of resource types in this registration (e.g., load_curtailment, battery_storage, solar_der, ev_charging, chp). Captures the composition of the portfolio for capacity credit calculations.

Required telemetry reporting interval in seconds (e.g., 4, 10, 60) for real-time performance monitoring. Null if telemetry is not required.
Indicates whether real-time telemetry data submission to the ISO/RTO is required for this registration. Typically required for Tier 1 performance resources and VPP registrations.

The average actual load curtailment in kilowatts (kW) achieved by the customer across all participated DR events during the current enrollment period. Used for program performance analysis and capacity planning.

Indicates whether day-of or morning-of baseline adjustments are enabled for this enrollment to account for real-time load variations. True: adjustments are applied. False: unadjusted baseline is used for M&V.

The methodology used to calculate the customer baseline load (CBL) for measurement and verification (M&V) of load curtailment performance. High X of Y: average of highest X days in prior Y days. Mid X of Y: average of middle X days in prior Y days. Regression: statistical regression model. Control Group: comparison to similar non-participating customers. The date on which the utility approved the customer's NEM agreement application.

business_glossary_term: Net Energy Metering (NEM) Effective End Date
description: The date on which the NEM agreement expires or is terminated. Null for open-ended agreements or active agreements without a defined end date.

business_glossary_term: Net Energy Metering (NEM) Effective Start Date
description: The date from which NEM billing and credit calculations begin. May differ from enrollment date if retroactive application is approved.
The date the customer was officially enrolled in the NEM program and the agreement became effective.
The per-kilowatt-hour (kWh) rate at which the customer is compensated for energy exported to the grid, expressed in dollars per kWh.

The methodology used to compensate the customer for energy exported to the grid. Retail Rate (full retail electricity rate, typical for NEM 1.0), Avoided Cost (utility's avoided cost of generation, typical for NEM 3.0), NEM Tariff Rate (specific tariff rate defined by PUC for NEM program), Wholesale Rate (market-based wholesale energy price).

The specific NEM program version under which the customer is enrolled. NEM 1.0 (legacy retail rate compensation), NEM 2.0 (retail rate minus non-bypassable charges), NEM 3.0 / NEM-ST (successor tariff with export compensation based on avoided cost), VNEM (Virtual Net Energy Metering for multi-tenant or community solar), NEMA (NEM Aggregation for multiple meters under single customer).
reference: CPUC Decision 14-03-041, CPUC Decision 16-01-044, CPUC Decision 21-12-006

description: For NEM Aggregation (NEMA) agreements, the type of meter aggregation allowed. Null for non-NEMA agreements.
The scheduled date of the next NEM true-up reconciliation for this agreement.
Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special conditions related to the NEM agreement.
The physical location description of the Point of Interconnection where the customer's DER system connects to the utility distribution grid.

Current lifecycle status of the NEM agreement. Pending (application submitted, awaiting approval), Active (agreement in force, customer receiving NEM credits), Suspended (temporarily inactive due to non-compliance or system outage), Terminated (customer or utility ended agreement), Expired (agreement reached end of term).

business_glossary_term: Self-Generation Incentive Program (SGIP) Incentive Eligible Flag

The date on which the NEM agreement was terminated by the customer or utility. Null if agreement is still active.
The reason for termination of the NEM agreement (e.g., customer request, system decommissioned, non-compliance, property sale). Null if agreement is still active.

The timestamp when this NEM agreement record was last modified in the system.

business_glossary_term: Virtual Net Energy Metering (VNEM) Allocation Method
description: For Virtual NEM (VNEM) agreements, the method by which generation credits are allocated among multiple beneficiary accounts. Proportional (based on each account's usage), Fixed Percentage (predefined allocation percentages), Custom (customer-defined allocation rules). Null for non-VNEM agreements.

description: FK to compliance.regulatory_agency.regulatory_agency_id — Rate cases are filed with specific PUCs or FERC. Essential FK for jurisdictional tracking.
Date the utility formally filed the rate case application with the regulatory commission. This date triggers statutory review timelines and procedural schedules.
reference: State PUC Filing Requirements; FERC Section 205 Filing Procedures

description: Regulatory jurisdiction where the rate case is filed. Use state abbreviation for state PUC filings (e.g., CA, TX, NY) or FERC for Federal Energy Regulatory Commission filings.
reference: FERC Jurisdiction; State PUC Jurisdictional Authority

description: Date the commission issued its final order deciding the rate case. This date establishes the effective date for new rates (subject to any suspension or phase-in provisions).
reference: State PUC Procedural Rules; FERC Rules of Practice and Procedure

description: Deadline for parties to file initial post-hearing briefs summarizing their positions and legal arguments. Null if case settled or procedural schedule not yet established.
reference: State PUC Procedural Rules; FERC Rules of Practice and Procedure

description: Number of parties granted intervenor status in the rate case proceeding, including consumer advocates, industrial customer groups, environmental organizations, and other stakeholders.
reference: State PUC Procedural Rules; FERC Rules of Practice and Procedure

description: Free-form text field for additional notes, observations, or context regarding the rate case proceeding that do not fit structured fields.
Date of the prehearing conference where procedural schedule, discovery protocols, and hearing logistics are established. Null if not yet scheduled or if case settled before hearing.
reference: State PUC Procedural Rules; FERC Rules of Practice and Procedure

description: Date the administrative law judge or hearing officer issued a proposed decision recommending findings and conclusions to the commission. Null if not applicable in this jurisdiction or if case settled.

Overall percentage increase in customer rates proposed by the utility in the initial filing, calculated as the change in revenue requirement divided by current revenues. Expressed as a percentage. Negative values indicate a rate decrease.
Timestamp when this rate case record was first created in the system.
Timestamp when this rate case record was last modified in the system.
Name of the regulatory affairs manager responsible for coordinating the utility's rate case strategy, filings, and stakeholder engagement.
Date the commission issued its decision on the rehearing petition. Null if no rehearing was requested or decision not yet issued.
reference: State PUC Procedural Rules; FERC Rules of Practice and Procedure

description: Date the petition for rehearing was filed. Null if no rehearing was requested.
reference: State PUC Procedural Rules; FERC Rules of Practice and Procedure

description: Indicates whether any party filed a petition for rehearing or reconsideration of the final commission order (True) or not (False).
reference: State PUC Procedural Rules; FERC Rules of Practice and Procedure

description: Deadline for parties to file reply briefs responding to initial briefs. Null if case settled or procedural schedule not yet established.
reference: State PUC Procedural Rules; FERC Rules of Practice and Procedure

description: Date the settlement agreement was executed by the parties. Null if the case was not settled.
reference: State PUC Settlement Procedures; FERC Settlement Policy

description: Indicates whether the rate case was resolved through a settlement agreement among the utility, commission staff, and intervenor parties (True) or proceeded to a fully litigated commission decision (False).
reference: State PUC Settlement Procedures; FERC Settlement Policy

description: Number of days the commission suspended the proposed rate effective date to allow time for review and hearing. Statutory suspension periods vary by jurisdiction (e.g., 5 months for FERC, varies by state PUC).
reference: Federal Power Act Section 205; State PUC Statutes

description: Ending date of the test year period used to establish the revenue requirement and cost of service.
reference: State PUC Rate Case Standards; FERC Cost of Service Methodology

description: Beginning date of the test year period used to establish the revenue requirement and cost of service. Test year may be historical, future, or a hybrid period depending on jurisdiction.
reference: State PUC Rate Case Standards; FERC Cost of Service Methodology

description: Unique identifier for each regulatory correspondence record. Primary key for the regulatory correspondence entity.
Foreign key reference to a related regulatory audit or inspection record if the correspondence is part of an audit process.

Foreign key reference to a related regulatory filing or submission if the correspondence is associated with a formal filing.

Email address of the regulatory agency contact person for follow-up and correspondence tracking.

description: Name of the regulatory agency representative or contact person who sent or is the recipient of the correspondence.
Phone number of the regulatory agency contact person.

description: Number of attachments or supporting documents included with the correspondence.
Date the Board of Directors or executive leadership was notified of the correspondence. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

Notes documenting the resolution or closure of the correspondence matter, including any final actions taken or outcomes achieved.

description: Categorical classification of the correspondence type. Business identifier or reference number assigned to the correspondence by the utility or regulatory body. Used for tracking and citation in regulatory proceedings.

Official docket number or proceeding reference assigned by the regulatory body. Used to link correspondence to specific regulatory cases, rate cases, or compliance proceedings.

description: File path, URL, or document management system reference where the physical or electronic correspondence document is stored.

Indicates whether additional follow-up action or supplemental response is required after the initial response.
Username or identifier of the system user who last modified the regulatory correspondence record.

description: Timestamp when the regulatory correspondence record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

description: Priority level assigned to the correspondence based on urgency, regulatory impact, and potential enforcement risk. Critical priority indicates immediate action required.
Name or title of the regulatory proceeding, case, or investigation to which this correspondence relates.

reference: NERC, FERC, State PUC, EPA, DOE, OSHA

description: Name of the internal department or business unit responsible for preparing the response. Examples include Legal, Regulatory Affairs, Compliance, Environmental, Operations, or Finance.
Date the regulatory agency acknowledged receipt of the utility's response. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Date by which a response is required or expected. Critical for tracking compliance with regulatory deadlines and avoiding enforcement actions. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: FERC Procedural Rules, State PUC Regulations

description: Current status of the response to the correspondence. Tracks whether a response is pending, in progress, completed, overdue, or not required.
Date the utility's response was submitted to the regulatory agency. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

Brief description of the subject matter or topic of the correspondence. Includes topics such as NERC CIP (Critical Infrastructure Protection) audits, FERC data requests, PUC rate case interrogatories, EPA emissions reporting, or DOE energy data submissions.

The date on which the regulatory agency formally approved or accepted the filing, if applicable.
The date on which the regulatory commission issued its final order or decision on this filing.
The official order number issued by the regulatory commission in response to this filing (e.g., FERC Order No. 1000, PUC Order No. U-20788-A).

description: For CPCN applications, the current status of required environmental impact assessments and regulatory reviews (e.g., NEPA, state environmental permits).

business_glossary_term: Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity (CPCN) Project Description
description: For CPCN applications, a detailed description of the proposed capital project, including scope, location, technology, and public benefit justification.
reference: State PUC CPCN Requirements; FERC Certificate Procedures

description: The timestamp when this regulatory filing record was first created in the system.

description: The regulatory deadline by which this filing must be submitted to remain in compliance.

description: A detailed narrative description of the purpose, scope, and content of this regulatory filing, including key issues addressed and relief requested.

description: The total number of supporting documents, exhibits, and attachments included in this regulatory filing submission.

description: The ending date of the reporting or compliance period covered by this filing.

description: The beginning date of the reporting or compliance period covered by this filing (e.g., start of fiscal year for FERC Form 1, start of quarter for EQR).
